Name: Adrian Bui
Testimonial:

She makes learning an enjoyable and addicting activity, through her encouraging methods and optimistic attitude towards her students. She assists each student in their needed area, by helping them revise and study for a test, helping them check and correct any errors along the way. Overall, with Miss Stephanie’s teachings and guidance, I find myself being more confident and well - rounded in school, being able to keep up and even stay ahead of my classmates in class, and this is all thanks to Miss Stephanie.
Adrian Bui - LaSalle Catholic College High School - Year 9

Name: Adrian Dang
Testimonial:
I’ve been at DA for 9 years now, and can say that the teachers are by far the best, helping me achieve new heights I wouldn’t have without their constant support and investment in my learning. Not only are they extremely caring, the teachers put an immense amount of hard work and dedication into helping us achieve exemplary results, which has created a comfortable learning environment for students.

I began in private classes in year 3 which helped build the foundation of my learning in the lead up to years 5 and 6, where I had Mr Danny, and without him and his constant support I would not have achieved excellent results in the selective exams.

Following this I had Miss Linda and Miss Jenny, and I owe them a large debt of gratitude in helping me excel in my junior years of high school, putting in an immeasurable amount of effort into making sure I’m prepared for exam conditions and submitting assignments on time and at a high standard.

From the lead up to my senior years of high school, I had Mr King and Miss Amanda as my maths teachers, and they are undoubtedly the best math tutors you will find. Having their constant support through ensuring students understand concepts thoroughly whilst exposing us to the toughest questions to prepare for exam conditions constituted my success, and not to mention their unique connection with students which help them understand our areas of improvement to help us excel.

For English advanced I had Miss Lai, and she is the most supportive, hardworking and dedicated teacher you will find, helping me thrive during assignments and exams which boosted my ranks by 10 places between semester 1 and 2, and topping my class for the trial exams.

I’ve enjoyed every moment here and highly recommend for anyone looking for a fantastic tutor :)

Name: Adrian Nguyen
Testimonial: Da tuition is a great place to learn, the teachers are open to questions and help all students achieve high marks. Basically guaranteed A's if you go here.

Name: Aiden
Testimonial: DA is a super good place to go to improve your marks, miss Linda helped me get high marks this term and is a great teacher

Name: Albert Tran
Testimonial:
Mr Bunsea is an awesome tutor who I was lucky enough to have to help me throughout the course of year 12 and the HSC. He is an extremely friendly and approachable tutor who was able to guide me through some of the most tedious and conceptually confusing 4U topics like conics and harder 3U, and even made me enjoy learning them. Whenever I was stuck on a difficult question in class, Mr Bunsea would always be able to help me understand it in a clear manner, allowing me to approach future questions in the same way. Even out of class he offered his help, as I was able to message him if I had any trouble with any questions, which was really useful especially during exam season and I greatly appreciated this. So it was with his help that I was actually able to get 100% on one of my 4U exams in year 12 at Baulkham Hills, as well as get the highest bands in both 3U and 4U for the HSC; and I can't thank him enough.
Albert Tran - Baulkham Hills High School - Year 12

Name: Alex Phan

He would always ensure that he helps you solve your mathematical problems at anytime even during the early hours of the mornings. He is always open arms towards his students and is a caring meme-y bloke. Honestly you are in the right hands if you have Mr. King as your tutor. 
Alex Phan - Liverpool Boys - Year 12

Name: Alex Sivannarith
Testimonial: mr danny and mr mark are good teachers who explained and elaborated on the tasks that i was doing in school which i struggled or needed help on they made it easy for me to understand it

Name: Alice Craz
Testimonial: I have visited several tutoring centers, but I am truly grateful that I joined DA during my HSC years. The tutors at DA have consistently been patient and supportive, and always going beyond to ensure that every student reaches their full potential. I joined DA just two weeks before the prelims, feeling extremely stressed about my lack of understanding in math. However, after being placed in Mr. Bunsea's private class, I discovered that studying math was far less terrible and stressful than I had imagined. Mr. Bunsea's guidance and support throughout year 12 helped me make significant improvements. Without his assistance, I would likely have dropped math advanced. While my results may not be the best, I have made such progress compared to where I was in year 11. I also enrolled in Mr. Danny's chemistry class in year 12 and felt much more confident approaching the subject with the knowledge he provided. I had always struggled to keep up in this subject, but with Mr. Danny's support, I managed to make positive progress. In addition to my main tutors, I would like to express my gratitude to Mr. King, Mr. Adem, Mr. Phillips, and Mrs. Helen for their excellent teaching and the study resources they provided, which will likely last me a lifetime. If you are hesitant about joining DA and are reading this, I urge you to sign up as soon as possible because time matters. I am sincerely thankful to all the tutors at DA – it truly is the best tutoring center ever! Byeeeeeee!

Name: Alison Tan
Testimonial:
There’s a quote that I hold to be very true - “A dream doesn’t become reality through magic; it takes sweat, determination and hard work.” This the very embodiment of what DA helps you to achieve. They take your abstract dreams and transform them into a tangible reality. 
The teachers at DA really take you under their wing. They have such a strong passion for teaching, an incredibly strong work ethic and are super approachable. They are also always there to help you, whether it be urgently needing test prep, collecting solutions for packages or even messaging them at 2 in the morning because you have endless queries on your test tomorrow. Okay please don’t do that often, Mr King will come for me. But trust me when I say; DA tutors take your education and place it as their top priority. Their sole objective is to be your stepping stone and to help you achieve your goal. Yes, there will be mountains of packages for you to climb over and a never-ending pile of past papers for you to complete, but everything you do will pay off. Although, I’ll give you a helpful tip to make life easier- always have mos on you. They should be your most prized possessions. 
Anyhow, glancing back from where I started, I am able to fully comprehend the upmost importance of DA. Like myself, you will witness first-hand the power of hard work and determination and will have garnered an essential life skill for you to take away. And there will be a moment, when you look back onto everything that DA has taught you, and you will realise that your dreams have finally become a reality.
Alison Tan - Cheltenham Girls High School - Year 11

Name: Althea Trieu
Testimonial:
Despite only recently joining DA, I can already tell that the environment here is less stressful compared to other tutors. You can tell that the teachers here genuinely care and are attentive towards their students needs. Before I joined, I never had the courage to ask questions in class, but because of my teachers friendly and understanding nature, it made it so much easier. Instead of dreading the day before, I actually want to attend my classes. They have not only significantly improved my understanding of certain subjects but also taught me that effectively working hard will eventually get me to where I want.
Althea Trieu - Cerdon College - Year 12

Name: Amanda Vu
Testimonial: I have been attending DA tuition for almost 4 years now, and I have never felt more accomplished. Throughout these years, I have seen immense improvement in my schooling results. Originally I attended DA’s selective training class through years 5 - 6, then made my way up to GAT class through year 7. Then, I climbed my way up to ET class through years 8 and currently 9, proving my abilities with top marks and satisfactory results. ET class with Miss Linda is by far the best class I’ve been a part of because of the comfortable environment, and having a knowledgeable teacher that encourages me to do well. Miss Linda’s teaching style is extremely clear and easy to perceive, allowing me to understand content easily, and to apply it tangibly. I love how motivational she is too, encouraging us students to strive to accomplish academic goals as well as enjoying our years of youth. Thanks to Miss Linda, I have become more confident in my abilities and have been more motivated in DA and at school, due to the satisfying results I have accomplished through her guidance. In school I have been able to rank top 5 in the grade for every maths test this year (95% and above) and I couldn't be happier :). As for DA, I have been improving each week, ranking high for homework results as well as in class quizzes. I'm excited for another year at DA!

I didn’t fully understand how to construct excellent essays, analyse texts critically or even participate in class. Through Miss Lai’s determination to help me do well, it has rubbed onto me and has made me enjoy English at school and understanding new concepts - I find myself more confident participating in class and writing essays. Miss Lai is also the kindest teacher you will ever come across. You may be feeling down or not so keen about your school marks and she will always be there to cheer you up ￼No matter how busy she is, she will always make time to help you with school assignments and even talk to you. Miss Lai knows English in and out which is why she helps me work super hard so that it pays off at the end ((:
Amber Nguyen - Prairiewood High School - Year 11

Name: Anh Nguyen
Testimonial:
Tutors are there to lead you to your personal success. Despite all the darkest time in your life, they'll be by your side "Don't give up". I was on the brink of losing all hope and pride in myself until my tutor gave me hope to believe in my goal. They feel pain when you cry and also know they've succeeded if you're happy. 
Anh Nguyen - Casimir Catholic College - Year 12

Name: Anna Karavaev
Testimonial:
When I first started my journey at DA, I did not develop the critical thinking until Ms Amanda and the other tutor teachers had pushed me into the right kind of mindset and mentality that I needed to further myself in getting the best marks possible. Great marks and high scores comes from consistency and accuracy, which is one of the main things DA emphasizes and had taught me from the very beginning. Miss Amanda made me realize the importance of maintaining a regular study pattern throughout high school which helped me cope well under the extreme pressures of Year 12, as well as achieving amazing accomplishments such as topping my subjects throughout year 11 and 12  then receiving 2nd and 3rd Dux of the Year awards for Year 11 and Year 12. I went from being an average student coming home with C’s on my report card to receiving consistent A’s and getting in the top 5 ranks for each of my subjects then achieving band 6 for both 2u and 3u mathematics in my HSC. Even though the countless booklets of DA homework may seem overwhelming, and Ms Amanda’s ‘speed and accuracy’ games can give you a heart attack when you’re being put on the spot or pressured under a (nearly impossible) time limit, you have to understand that in the end all the hard work and efforts eventually pay off! Work hard and also cherish the times you have with DA, I can tell you now that you’ll miss your DA family (and definitely the drinks) once you’ve finished school. Good luck in the years to come, it’ll all be worth it in the end.
Anna Karavaev - Bossley Park High School - Year 12

Name: Anna Le
Testimonial:
English can appear frivolous but understanding the core of it is realising the power of language. My English tutor Miss Selina has been beside me throughout every assessment task and has taught me in ways that have allowed me to attain not just a good rank but an appreciation of the essence of English. I’ve learnt that it is more a process of attrition rather than epiphany where the reason why English is learnt can be discovered.
Anna Le - Prairiewood High School - Year 11

Name: Anna Le
Testimonial: Mr Jacob was my tutor at DA. He was always actively encouraging me to push myself to do the best. When it came to my exam, he offered a one on one exam prep classes which i found really helpful and was able to achieve much higher marks in my exam and improve my rank. I’m very helpful because i wouldn’t have been able to achieve these marks without him

Name: Anna Pham
Testimonial:
DA changed my life in a way nothing else had. My eight year journey at DA transformed me - not only in my studies, but in my attitude and confidence. Miss Amanda is the most wonderful and inspiring teacher I have had, the one who pushed me when I was complacent, encouraged me when I was disappointed and had high hopes for me like no one else. She is the one who had guided me to achieve my 100% in both 2unit and 3unit in my HSC, and was ranked 4th in the state for Maths. Her constant brain training and speed and accuracy games were by far the best part of the lesson, despite the heart attacks they induced when you were racing to put your pen down first. This, along with all her gangster methods and stories about sharks, cats with their trash and treasure and square boys missing out on four awesome chicks, had allowed me to strive to reach my full potential and receive the results that I had. She allowed me to believe that hard work trumps all, that even coming from some disadvantaged school ranked somewhere in the 200s did not mean I couldn’t achieve great results. Her strict high expectations and no-sooking attitude pushed me to perform at 110%, always aiming to get 100% for every exam. Her methods of teaching made even the most difficult concepts appear effortlessly easy and you wouldn’t know it until hours later and you were attempting it by yourself. She had made learning memorable and enjoyable. I also appreciated all those times in class where we deviated from work and she was simply giving each student life advice, because you could tell she wasn’t just a tutor - she cares. She truly was a miracle to me. So let DA become your miracle - work with your tutor, try hard and don’t become discouraged when you fall. Because your tutor will help you on the way up and together you can climb even the steepest of mountains together. PS. Miss Amanda really isn’t as scary as the rumours say (I was not paid to say this). Also shoutout to Miss Lai, Miss Linda, Mr King, Mr Phi and Miss Michelle.
Anna Pham - Mary Mackillop Catholic School - Year 12
Theme: State Ranking Could Also Be Your Miracle

Name: Anna Tran
Testimonial: I haven’t been in DA for long, but I already see a difference in my learning. DA tuition helped my motivation in studying to work harder to achieve your goal. Miss Stephanie is a great tutor that has helped me improve so much throughout only one term. Overall, DA has assisted me in pursuing my goals and enhance my learning capabilities.

Name: Anthony Nguyen
Testimonial: I've been attending Da Tuition since Year 10, starting with Maths and later adding English and Biology in Years 11 and 12. Their dedication and tailored teaching have truly boosted my confidence and inspired me to constantly improve, especially in Maths, where I was able to achieve 2nd place out of 135.

Name: Arwa Amir

Maybe it's because every lesson I learn so many new things because of his excellent skills when it comes to explaining concepts; or maybe it's the way that he cares for his students wellbeing by greeting us with smiles every lesson and the fact that he makes consistent efforts to genuinely talk to us about our lives. That's the thing about my classes with Mr Bunsea, they're never one sided, he puts in as much effort as he can to ensure we understand what we're being taught.

I'm truly glad to have been taught by a considerate and talented individual like himself, it's safe to say that he has left a significant impact on my academic life and has never failed to act as someone I can look up to.

DA provides a fun and friendly working environment which allows students to thrive and perform work to the best of their abilities. Overall, a great tuition centre.

Name: Aryan Sharma
Testimonial:
I don’t know how to express these feelings but I will try my best. My maths tutor Mr Bunsea he is such a wonderful and loving soul the way he explains maths is so funny and easy. He breaks down every single question in a way that is nothing’ Even I would give up but he is the pushing me forward and encouraging me saying-‘Aryan you got this’ ‘you can do it’. This only lead to my improvement and betterment in maths where i went from dropping Maths Advanced as a subject to regretting why I didn’t choose extension!
Secondly My Biology and Chemistry tutor Mr Danny omg he is so interesting and intelligent person. He was a missing character in my life who encouraged me all through out my year 12. I was very nervous first few lessons but everything changed after those lessons. He not only gives us knowledge about biology and chemistry but also gives us lessons about life. His approach to study is different and that makes him the most interesting person at Da tution. He even educated us about our carrier paths and told us those pro’s and con’s of those fields which lead me decide my future carrier’. The improvement in my results were immaculate and I am so grateful for him and his help!! In our term three we were accompanied by Mr Adam he’s so genuine and his approach made me feel like home. Where his friendly nature and young age made me feel like i am learning from my friend. I have only experienced this at Da tution.
Thirdly and not lastly Miss Kassandra my English was not so great but she made me felt i can do it !Her help through out this year was needed and she have been a great listener and helped me going out of her schedule. Tbh no one does that but at Da this us possible. Miss have explained me the easiest and hardest concepts in a matter of moments and my marks west from 5/20 to 18/20. Even in trials there were massive improvements under time pressure as well.
DA have been my second home and i am grateful because of these mentioned names and some other present there.
Thank-you DA family for all the love and help.
I wish i could add more stars to this review!!

Name: Ayah Karaki

"You look really tired, go splash some water on your face and you will feel better." I remember being told this by my fantastic tutor Mr Bunsea during my first lesson of "Parametric". I was exhausted and I didn't believe that doing such a simple action could amount to anything, but for some reason I picked myself up anyway and did it. Surprisingly, it worked as I noticed after returning to my seat once more. This story is linked to my journey with Mathematics. I was not performing to a satisfactory level and it was not just me that noticed this, my mother did too. In search of a solution, she asked around and was suggested "DA Tuition". I was sceptical at first, but decided to try it out anyway, after all, there was nothing to lose. At once, I noticed an improvement in my mathematical knowledge and how I applied them that was reflected in the increase in marks I got at school, and it is with sincere thanks to the tutors of this great institution. It is apparent through their dedication and motivation that they strive for your success in the subjects you come here for. Not only that, but they also nurture your enjoyment as they are fun people who do not mind holding a light-hearted conversation with you. If you, the reader, care to take a lesson from this wall of text, it would be to give a sincere attempt at anything you are learning. Once you become exhausted, consult the tutors of this institution for assistance and I can guarantee that you will acquire a firm grasp of the concept in no time. And after that if you are exhausted, go splash some water on your face to refresh yourself and continue your journey towards academic success!
Benjamin Chen - Patrician Brothers High School - Year 12

Name: boogie woogie man
Testimonial: I've always been insecure and unconfident about my English skills. However, since beginning tutoring with Miss Lai, I have drastically improved with both my skills and understanding. Name: Brandon Voraboud
Testimonial:

But then when it mattered the most in my senior years I lost my motivation, didn't want to study, didn't want to attend tutor at all, and the days I did I would just sit there on my phone and not even worry about my work. Name: Christina Lee
Testimonial:
After finishing my selective program I was offered a place in Miss Lai' s GAT English class and it was overwhelming as I didn't think I excelled in English. When I joined i realised I would be learning alongside others in a grade above and it was daunting, I didn't believe I was good enough and others would judge me. However as time progressed, Miss Lai was able to confidently shine a new light upon me and made me realise I was capable of analysing challenging texts, reading articles and creating solid responses. She consistently works hard with a smile to motivate and support her students and bring out creativity and optimism in everyone. When entering high school, I saw reading books as boring and useless but she always reminded us how important it was and introduced her students to fascinating books and broadened our perception of reading through articles, poems and short stories. I have now learnt to appreciate reading shorter novels and listening to speech poetry. I used to receive average marks around 70% but with Miss Lai' s repetitive reminder to be on top of my work, edit and proof-read, I worked my way up towards 80% and received my first 90% in year 9. Some lessons she came in with stacks of vocabulary, unexpected memory quizzes, speed writing paragraphs and i never knew how much it would help me. I was able to memorise my 900 word essay in 2 nights and executed writing it all in 40 minutes without skipping a beat. I remember complaining about her challenging us to write faster or think outside the box but now I look forward to her little tests as I know she thinks ahead and only prepares her students for later adversities which we'll then find easy. Even when deadlines became a blur she made sure I wouldn't get lost and pulled me aside for a small chat which always stuck with me and made me realise there was no point agonising and dwelling over things when I could learn from my mistakes, lift my head up and achieve greater heights. After studying under Miss Lai for 4 years she has brought out my love for English and has inspired me to stay 
determined and passionate towards my work. Her charismatic personality in class encourages a fun, bright and collaborative environment and i will forever appreciate her being my teacher and look forward to going through senior years under her guidance.

Also no matter what Miss Linda or Miss Amanda do or how far they split you up, your cousins will never become your enemy.

I am genuinely grateful for the positive influence Mr Bunsea and Miss Anna had on my education.

Name: Damian Do
Testimonial:
Helpful. Out of all of the words that could be used to describe this tuition in its simplest form, helpful. This place has assisted me with the advancement of my education, managed my time efficiency that could be used for meaningful events outside of school. This tutor actually contains teachers that actually know what the topic is and how to teach it. In my own opinion, this tuition is a very underrated and selfless mini-school. For those pondering if they should go to or take their child to this facility, then they should only come if they want what is best for themselves or child.

First of all, DA Tuition is located within Canley Heights, meaning that it is filled with asians. If that isn't a sign that they excel in teaching and education, then I don't know what is. The millions of dollars that is funded towards schools that aim to improve our learning is third-rate in comparison to DA Tuition. The dungeon that they place us in has exponentially increased my learning. Throughout my experience within this tutoring service, I discovered why they do not relocate to bigger buildings, and it is because if they did then the world's population might be too heavily one-sided within Canley Vale, Australia in contrast to the rest of the world. In school, it is easy to get distracted from using  electronics, talking to friends, or just daydreaming. However, DA Tuition strongly radiates a learning environment. The years I have spent there, and I can't recall a single time that I didn't do work or didn't learn.

I first came to this tutor because I needed help with mathematics after my new high school maths teacher stated that "In order to do well in maths, you have to become one with it, like a romantic relationship." It was then realised that I wasn't going to learn anything from him. First entering, I expected it to be like an extra school period on the weekend. I couldn't have been more wrong. Mr Bunsea (my mathematical husband) welcomed me with welcome arms and treated me with the care that a mother would do. I came to DA to be with my friend as well, but after only a few lessons, I wasn't there to see him anymore and rather I was actually excited to learn.

The unique characteristics of this tuition that crowns DA Tuition as the best tutoring facilities within Australia all come down to one factor, they care for you. And I'm certain that many other teachers have said those words before, but this place is different. The few people that are present during a session represents the commitment that the teachers make to helping each specific student in their learning signifies how much they care for us. I feel personally that I am closer to my tutoring teacher rather than even some of my friends. Schools and other tutors commonly pressures their students to do well, whilst here at DA, we are inspired instead. The countless hours that my teacher has personally spent to make sure that I am ready for a test has been emotionally moving for me. Providing series of extra lessons so that they can help you learn more and revise without being charged, illustrating the open-hearted and selfless aura that the tuition emits.

It is through the commitment of DA Tuition that I was able to improve my grade from a C at 63%, to an A at 94% within maths advanced. My achievements have only been capable due to the willingness of this tuition and Mr Bunsea and was able to pass a maths exam in which most of my peers failed to do.

Out of all of the choices in my life, going to DA Tuition was the smartest thing that I have ever done, without them I would be at a loss. If Harry Potter was a real person, I would be certain that he would've gone to this tuition. During my experience, I have seen people of various ages, from children to high school students like myself, and different ethnic groups. No matter the circumstances, I have always seen them with. smile, and for Mr Bunsea, every encounter has always included a laugh. So if you seek out a proper education, if you need a friend, and delicious restaurants across the street, come to DA Tuition.

Her care and enthusiasm for English are truly contagious, making every lesson an engaging and enriching experience. She has a remarkable ability to break down complex literary concepts into understandable, relatable ideas, ensuring that I not only grasp the material but also develop a genuine love for it. Ms. Kassandra’s dedication to her students’ success is evident in the personalised feedback and encouragement she provides, helping us all to grow both academically and personally. Her creative teaching methods and enthusiasm for the subject make her an invaluable asset to any student fortunate enough to learn from her. Mr. Bunsea is a true master of Extension 2 Maths, known for his exceptional skill in making even the most challenging concepts accessible to his students. His deep understanding of the course is matched only by his ability to convey this knowledge in a clear, concise, and inspiring manner. Mr. Bunsea’s patience and dedication to his students are unparalleled; he is always willing to go the extra mile to ensure that everyone fully comprehends the material. His lessons are not just about solving problems but about fostering a deep appreciation for the beauty and logic of mathematics. Students under his guidance consistently achieve outstanding results, a testament to his expertise and teaching excellence. Mr. King is an exceptional E1 Maths teacher who brings both passion and precision to his teaching. His ability to simplify the hardest advanced and extension 1 content and present it in a funny and understandable way is truly remarkable. Mr. King’s enthusiasm for the subject is infectious, motivating his students to push their boundaries and achieve their best. He is incredibly supportive, always encouraging me to think critically and approach problems with confidence. Mr. King’s dedication to his craft and his students is evident in the way he tailors his teaching to meet the unique needs of each individual, ensuring that everyone in his class reaches their full potential. His impact on my academic journey is profound. These teachers have clearly made a significant positive impact on my academic journey, and their dedication and passion deserve the highest praise.

There is something special with DA that one cannot find elsewhere. Teachers that genuinely care about the progress of their students. DA knows the secret to success; it is the constant support and motivation, the valuable life lessons that are taught have

Over the next few months I had found myself accustomed to studying every day and eventually my grades and attitude towards education and life took a 360 turn. I was no longer a thorn in my teacher’s side, I enjoyed going to school and tutor and when I look back at my time in year 11 and 12, it was filled with bright memories and success. Anyone and everyone can find that special something in DA.

Name: Diana El Safadi
Testimonial:
I just want to start off by saying that DA tuition is certainly one of a kind. The friendships I've made and the laughs I've had will truly never be forgotten. The tutors welcomed me with open arms and have guided me to success. Collectively, they've supported me and helped me through my senior years and I wouldn't have made it without them. In particular, I'd like to acknowledge Mr Danny, Ms Lai and Mr Bunsea for their tremendous hard work and effort in everything they do.

Mr Danny was my super duper funny biology tutor who had the loudest voice that anyone could hear from a mile away. He really knew how to make the class laugh. Even though we complained soooooo much, he got through every lesson and made sure we were up to date with our work while still making the class enjoyable with stories and drama. He supported us and encouraged us to do our best. He always saw potential in us and never ever gave up on us. I always had problems with Biology, as science wasn't really my thing but he always knew when I was confused about something, and wouldn't hesitate to ask if I understood the concept. He always went out of his way to answer emails and a million questions super late at night. He gives the best advice about life and he's always genuinely interested in what we want to do with our lives. Overall, he nurtured my curiosity and helped me see the beauty in every cell, organism, and ecosystem. He is definitely the best biology tutor I've ever had with a big heart who always put his students first.

I had Ms Lai for Advanced English and she has the most beautiful soul I've ever seen in a person. Her passion for English is truly incredible. She can analyse any type of photograph or text in an instant and it still suprises me to this day. Her writing abilities are like none I've ever seen before, and she has done an amazing job at teaching me how to be a good writer like her. Throughout my senior years, I stayed ranked top 3 with her help, and she made sure it didn't move. My love for English continued to prosper every time I worked with her and our late nights editing 6 page essays will never be forgotten. She put in so much effort to check all my answers and drafts, no matter what day or time it was. Every time she saw me, she never forgot to ask how I was feeling or how everything was going. Overall, she was my guiding light on my literary journey and her sweet personality is rare to find in a person. She definitely has a gift for changing kids lives.

Last but not least, the amazing Mr Bunsea, my Standard Maths tutor. He definitely had so much patience dealing with all my complaining about how much I hate maths. He always told me that by the end of my schooling years, I would like Maths better than English, but its good to see his persistence and resilience to try even though English is obviously a way better subject. In all seriousness, he had so much dedication in helping me improve because I was sitting at an average of 50 in year 11 and now I'm sitting at a 70. I always thought I would fail in this subject, but he always had faith that I'd do really well. That belief in me from him was the only thing that kept me going. When no one else believed I could do it, he did. He would always let me come in for extra hours, sometimes for the whole entire day just to do maths. The way he explains certain concepts was really well done. He slowly goes through it and makes sure I fully understand before going on to the next thing. When I made silly mistakes, I wouldn't only feel disappointed, but he would feel it too. Overall, his passion for numbers and dedication to teaching made math feel like a breeze. He made the world of equations and formulas so much more enjoyable.

I REALLY RECOMMEND COMING TO DA TUITION. These tutors treat you like their own children. I'm so thankful and grateful for them all, and couldn't have asked for better tutors. Its been a hectic journey with them all, and I'm so honoured to have experienced this journey with them :)

Name: Diana Nguyen
Testimonial:
DA Tuition provides a fun, and friendly environment, as well as encouraging teachers that will help you improve your grades from a C to an A.

Although I did have previous tutoring with private tutor and Ngo & Sons, they did not cater in the topics I needed help in or preparation for my exams. Other tutoring places did not motivate me and I honestly hated Mathematics for years, but ever since I started off at DA, each lesson would really open up my eyes and I started enjoying Maths. It eventually became therapeutic for me, it gave me satisfaction being able to overcome being able to overcome a problem. Miss Linda was the first teacher I had, she knows exactly what type of person her student is, she understands and reads her students clearly. 
Now that I am in year 10, it is my last year having her and she has taught me many skills I will definitely take on for the many years to come even if she isn't my tutor. Miss Linda is an inspirational and amazing tutor that is loved and respected by any student or parent.
She is very organised with all of her lessons, which aid to all of her students' needs, she also gives greats tips for memorising long for formulas and makes lessons fun and engaging, making 2 hours fly by so fast. She is a caring person that looks out for all of her students' well being and health, and sets realistic goals and expectations for each of her students  she is a great life mentor and I personally look up to her dearly. Without knowing she has affected my life in an extremely positive way, academically and mentally/emotionally. With Miss Linda's help, I have now been able help, understand, memorise and reteach my classmates, this not only allows my classmates to learn but for me to see that I full comprehend the skill I have used in application in that topic.

Name: Duc Tu
Testimonial:

Overall, I am extremely grateful to DA for developing such an amazing learning environment as I have really grown to love maths again and look forward to attending class each week.
10/10 would recommend ! ! !

Name: Elaine Nguyen
Testimonial:
Class by class, attending Miss Linda’s class was a pleasure where she was accepting and loving. Standing there every class thriving us to succeed beyond our dreams and limits. From these past term, I have noticed that my main subject had tremendously improved for the better, better grades and a new fresh look to my education. When struck with a problem, Miss Linda was always there to explain in detail. Answering our needs and supporting us through our journey. Excelling with school test results and reports, I have improved enormously where I started as a C student and now I’m getting 100% consecutively and ranking 1st in class this year in maths for all my topic tests! Meeting Miss Linda every week has increased my confidence whenever attempting a question allowing me to get these questions correct every time.
Elaine Nguyen - Canley Vale High School - Year 9

Name: Elisha Trinh
Testimonial: I started DA tuition around the middle of last year and I have been improving so much with my grades. Miss Stephanie, my English teacher, has given me many advice and help throughout my time here and I loved every moment of it. Not only is DA tuition only a tutoring place but they are a safe environment where every kid can enjoy coming to and all of the teachers are friendly towards everyone.

Name: Ella Carey
Testimonial: I came back to tutoring after covid and the teacher Miss Anna really helped me get back into learning at encouraged me to do my best. I am now in Year 10 and doing the best i’ve ever did. Thankyou Miss Anna

Name: Ellie Dang

I came here for 8 years and can confidently say that DA tuition is the best. All the tutors go above and beyond, investing so much time and effort into our learning. I have loved coming here, would 100% recommend !

Name: Emily
Testimonial:
DA tuition is special place where the teaches/staff strive to improve their students knowledge and marks as well as giving them the motivation to excel. I am currently in year 8 under the care of Miss Linda. She has helped improve my academic performance as well as my passion for learning. edit/update:
1 year later, I am now In year 9 under the care of Miss Stephanie for english. I have had miss Stephanie for many years and I can finally say I have grown and improved substantially in my english performance and results. Miss Stephanie's dedication and devotion to her students during assessment periods is just 🤯. I am very grateful for all her help and cannot wait to see what the future holds. If you need an English tutor, you MUST definitely go to DA Tuition as all their teachers are lovely, including the very kind Miss Stephanie :3.

Name: Emily Nguyen
Testimonial: I've been going to DA tuiton since year 5, and I can't explain how much this place has helped me improve academically throughout the years. My experience here has been great and the tutors here have helped me to continuously improve in school & achieve high marks. I used to be only an above-average kid, but with the help of Miss Linda and Miss Lai, my test results are now in the high 90's, plus getting 2nd place in maths in my grade & 100% in my recent test. My confidence in learning has improved significantly and now I'm determined in achieving above 90% for all my tests. I can't thank DA plus the teachers enough for their expertise and engaging lessons. With these incredible teachers & the great learning environment I'm looking forward to continue my journey here !

Name: Emily Vo
Testimonial:
DA tuition has become a second home to me over the past few years. This tutor provides me with so much support and motivation to do well in my studies and it is thanks to all the positive teachers surrounding the environment, namely my current tutors, Mr Danny, Mr King and Miss Kassandra. Mr Danny has given biology a fun and engaging name, leading me to feel comfortable in constantly asking questions and inspiring me to always achieve excellence in my work. He has helped me drastically in achieving the best marks possible within biology. Mr King has been my math tutor for several years, and despite the struggles, he has never given up on me and continues to do his best to help me in any way he can. He gives me the courage to keep trying and to persist with my mathematical studies. Miss Kassandra only started tutoring me right before my prelims in year 11. Although that period was stressful, especially with a new tutor, Miss Kassandra assisted me tremendously in preparation for the english examination, allowing me to get good marks despite not being present at the start of year 11. She has continued to be a huge help to me during my year 12 journey.

These tutors, among others, have all made my highschool experience incredibly enjoyable and a little less scary, giving me advice and support to achieve my best in my studies. They make learning fun, encouraging me and many other students to come into the centre, despite not having classes, just to study. They have undoubtedly shaped my attitude towards school and have had an unimaginable impact on my perseverance. I wouldn't be where I am now without them.

Name: Emma
Testimonial: I have only just recently started at DA Tuition, but I have seen large improvements in my personal understanding of study content due to the great help of my english tutor Miss Kathleen. She motivates her students to do their best and has faith in their abilities. She is friendly, kind and passionate about teaching, as her efforts are always consistent every lesson.

Name: Emma Thomas
Testimonial:
Having just completed the math HSC a few weeks ago I can confidently say that joining DA was the best decision I made during my senior years. When initially enrolling at DA term 2 of year 11 I was unsure what to expect as I had never received tutoring before. I had heard plenty of stories from some of my friends about negative experiences with other tutors which made me feel a bit apprehensive. Contrary to my expectations, I found DA to be an overwhelmingly welcoming place with exceptionally friendly and helpful staff. In fact, DA is home to some of the most experienced, hardworking and caring teachers you will ever encounter.

This was especially true for Mr Bunsea who was my tutor throughout years 11 and 12. Working with Mr Bunsea was an enjoyable experience and had both a calm and encouraging attitude that made me feel very comfortable. Moreover, from being taught by him I could tell that he truly understood and knew what he was doing. This was evident in the he was able to explain concepts that stumped me in a way that was nice and easy to understand. As well as his humorous approach of linking real-life examples with math concepts that made them easier to remember. I also appreciated how he always tried to make every student feel comfortable and welcomed as well as providing them with the best support possible. Moreover, towards the end of year 12, I had the privilege of being taught by both Mr Bunsea and Ms Anna who was also a great teacher and was very supportive and great at explaining.

Thanks to the support I received at DA, my grades in math substantially changed over the last two years. When I started DA, math was my weakest subject by far. Before DA, I had never once gotten an A or even B in math despite performing quite well in my other subjects. Through DA I was able to find a newfound confidence in math that wasn’t previously there. It was to the point that in my year 12 teacher-parent interview, my mum informed my teacher about how I started doing tutor mid-way through year 11. However, my teacher wasn’t surprised at all, and mentioned how she could tell just by my increasing confidence in maths that I showed in class, which surprised me. Unsurprisingly, my rise in confidence happened as a result of my increase in results. Before joining DA in the first term of year 11, I was scoring in the low 70s, however after joining DA this slowly began to change and by the end of year 12, I was scoring in the 90s which amazed me as someone who has never anyway near 90 before.

What went beyond all my expectations was my final ranking in 1st by the end of year 12. Throughout the year I was up against multiple math advanced students who had dropped to standard which made me truly doubt my chances of even getting in the top 5. However, it was through consistent effort and ongoing support from my tutors I was able to slowly close the gap between me and the top students. It truly felt like I was experiencing this journey alongside my tutors as we eagerly waited for my results for my trials and the end of the year as well as my final ranking. Which after much anticipation completely exceeded my expectations with me taking the rank of 1st at the end of year 12. Overall, I am extremely grateful for my experience at DA and for the memories and valuable support that I received, especially from Mr Bunsea and Ms Anna, who accompanied me on this incredibly rewarding journey.

Name: Ethan Hua
Testimonial: Mr Danny is my favourite teacher because he motivates me to try my best even if I’m struggling he pushes me to my limits and with this motivation I get full marks.

Name: Ethan Nguyen
Testimonial: This is the best tutoring centre ever. The teachers are very nice, they are funny, friendly, and opened minded. I have improved greatly thanks to Miss Amanda, Mr King, Miss Stephanie and Mr Bunsea.

Name: Eva Tran
Testimonial: I have Miss Kassandra for English, and she really helped my grade and my confidence in the subject. She's really supportive and always finds time to check over and edit my drafts. My marks have since improved from a B to an A range. I also have Mr Bunsea for maths adv and I am top 5 in my cohort. He gave me a lot of advice and guidance to allow me to reach this goal. Thank you!!

Name: Faithful Lilac
Testimonial: DA Tuition is an amazing tutoring with friendly teachers and classmates. It makes learning fun too! I highly recommend this as this will most definitely improve your marks and grades altogether. All of the past tutorings I went to usually only cared about the students learning, but DA Tuition is actually really friendly too, and won't get mad if you can't understand a concept.

Name: Felix Pittiglio
Testimonial: I've been taking English tuition at DA Tuition with Mrs. Jenny for 1 term now, and I've seen a huge improvement in the subject of English. She's a great teacher who explains things clearly and especially assists with any queries about my upcoming assessments. She has enabled a higher confidence in written exams and has significantly improved my grades in English.

Name: Filora Nguyen
Testimonial: DA tuition is excellent. My teachers Mr Danny and Mr Bunsea encourage and help me thrive academically. Would 100% recommend for student looking for a friendly environment and fantasic staff.

Name: Florence Nguyen
Testimonial: I’ve been coming to DA for around a year now, and it’s helped raise my grades tremendously !! Recently, I even scored full marks on my English assessment task thanks to Miss Stephanie, and am doing well in maths now with Miss Anna’s help. I’m really grateful for all the help and advice my teachers have given me about the HSC and subjects, and think that enrolling your child here would truly benefit them

Name: Gavin Tu
Testimonial:
Tutor is like family, we are here looking after one another. I thought I wasn't good enough but tutor showed me that if I put in the work and have a good support network, I can conquer anything, which is exactly what I did with the care of my tutors.
Gavin Tu - Prairiewood High School - Year 12

Name: Grace Tan
Testimonial:
When I first started coming to DA, I was very skeptical that it was going to be like all the other tutoring centres I have been to before. However, I soon realised that DA was the most unique and special tuition out there. Miss Linda really did everything in her power to help me reach my fullest potential. All of the extra hours, pop quizzes and homework in the past year definitely paid off. I started with 74% and gradually worked it up to the HIGH 90s. I couldn’t be more grateful for everything Miss Linda and DA has done for me. Have faith in your tutor, and one day you will look back to see how far you have come with their guidance. 
Grace Tan - Cheltenham Girls High School - Year 8

Name: Grace Tan
Testimonial:
I've been coming to DA every term for the last 8 years and cannot recommend it enough.

Through the tumultuous times from late primary school to the very end of year 12, the tutors at DA have been welcoming, patient, understanding and exceedingly well-versed in their fields. During the 2000+ hours I've spent within DA walls, I was consistently encouraged to push myself beyond my own limitations and expectations.

Aside from the exceptional tutors, DA possesses the most in-depth and beneficial study materials I've encountered, especially those for senior mathematics students. Their materials are specifically designed to accomodate the weaknesses of individual students. Particularly, their 'selective' and 'BOS' booklets are a collection of past paper questions from a myriad of sources which specifically relate to a specific topic, which I found personally beneficial for my learning.

Thank you to Mr Danny and Miss Stephanie for coaching me through the selective test, for Miss Linda for the laughs from years 7 to 10, for Mr King, whose teaching methods saved my year 11 grades, and for Mr Bunsea and Miss Autumn, who supported me at the very end.

It's the kind of place that makes graduating a little more bittersweet.

(p.s. miss Linda I've thought about it and I've decided to leave my mo's to my younger cousins- I figured they would need it more since they can't charge people for cups. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯)

Name: Gus Karaki
Testimonial:
Over the past term, Miss Kass has taught me so much about English, helping me with my English at school and at DA Tuition. Last year, I was receiving 80% and less, but now, I am consistently achieving over 90% in my English assessments and getting good grades. She is very consistent with helping me with my drafts when I need it, and gives me good feedback and advice regarding how to achieve the best results for my assessments. She makes sure she goes through my school topics with me thoroughly and makes sure I understand it fully.

Miss Lai has also been a great tutor in helping me with my marks and giving me good feedback for my assignments. She always teaches me new things about English and provides new insight into the topics I learn at school. Miss Kass and Miss Lai have been the best English tutors I’ve ever had. They are extremely helpful, resourceful and motivating.

Name: Halle Lam
Testimonial: DA tuition is a really great and friendly place. Miss Linda really helped me out with my math and allowed me to achieve full marks on my most recent test and all As on my other maths tests

Name: Hamza Jamil
Testimonial: I joined English tutoring half way through year 12 and maths tutoring a few weeks before. Mr King and Ms Amanda have helped improve my knowledge on Math concepts which has made it so much easier to apply them with the different questions I can get. By making booklets filled with past questions and every type of question really prepares us for our tests. To say I’m not an English student would be an understatement. Hence, when I started English tutoring, I was skeptical at how much I would actually learn. But, from the very first lesson, Ms Kassandra has been engaging and helpful. She is able to push me to reach my full potential even when i’m unmotivated. She has been supportive not only during our classes but outside them as well and is always there for her students. In fact, all the tutors are responsive outside of their normal working hours, which is a must for a senior student in their final year of high school.

Name: Hanna
Testimonial: As a year 9 student who was doing okay in maths with 60%’s , Miss Helen definitely boosted my grades to 85%+ whilst teaching me step by step so i understood the concepts. Miss Helen is a really nice teacher who helps her students when help is needed. Miss Helen has helped me top my class and I appreciate Miss Helen for that.

Name: Harrison Neal
Testimonial:
English has always been a subject I have enjoyed and I was pretty good at it. As the years went on and things got harder I started to enjoy the subject less and less and as a result, my marks dropped drastically. Every time I saw a low mark I received for English I would always become so frustrated to the point I practically gave up and allowing my marks to drop further. This had such an effect on me that this mindset basically rolled onto all my other subjects causing me to lose faith in myself and others.
With the help of Miss Lai however, I was able to regain motivation and willingness to succeed in not just English but in all my subjects. In the most difficult of times, she was there for me and encouraged me to persist through all my challenges big and small even when I thought they were impossible. There are a million things I could tell you about how Miss Lai has helped me but let me tell you the result instead.
Firstly, I am improving in English both in marks and rank, secondly, due to the increased improvement I have become more motivated and thirdly, most importantly my burning ambition and passion for English and the enjoyment of the subject has been restored. Overall making me happier. Therefore, I am eternally grateful to Miss Lai for everything she has done for me and more.
P.S. Just ‘IBIS’ your problems!
I - (I)
B - (Believe)
I - (In)
S - (School)
Harrison Neal - Thomas Hassall Catholic College - Year 11

Name: Harrison Neal
Testimonial:
Now I won’t lie I’ve always hated the idea and thought of tutoring, I always thought things like; “Hey I'm smart why can't I do this on my own,” or “It's worthless and it's not gonna help me improve.” Until I came to D.A. now I know it sounds silly but this place has had the most amazing effect on me. 
The tutors and students all made me feel like I am a part of a bigger family. I used to hate the idea of tutoring now I practically wanna come every, single day and stay for as long as possible. This place has been like a refuge to me in times of trouble in my life. Being there makes everything seem 100% better every single time I come here.
To students reading this who might have been in a similar position as I am or have been, let me tell you this right now. This place is one of the best things that ever happened to you. It's a true blessing. The people here are truly saints and are always here for you no matter how difficult you think things can be. 
I see so many people take this place for granted and I hope you realise that this place is like no other and the people here are one of a kind. This is coming from a kid who grew up all over the world seeing everything. Don’t waste your time here; take advantage and enjoy it because I know I’ll miss this place once I finish Grade 12.
Harrison Neal - Thomas Hassall Catholic College - Year 11

Name: Harrison Nguyen
Testimonial:
Extra study. Sounds like the worst idea in the world. Why would you put yourself through countless hours of revision and endless amounts of questions? The answer is simple really. Because hard work is necessary to success. But hard work must be guided because there is no point working hard at the wrong things. That’s where the tutors at DA step in. The tutors are there to help, support and guide you to success. Their unending commitment to you is what sets them apart. Sure, you’ll have to struggle through an untold amount of work, but when you get the marks you always wanted, you’ll look back and see that it was all worth it. I started at DA in year 5. Seems like a lifetime ago that I sat there, nervous about my first maths lesson. But I was wrong to be nervous, because the teachers were so supportive, friendly and reassuring. Over the years they have developed a relationship with each and every student. This personalised approach is a ‘sine’ of a great teacher and makes the whole experience so much better. But what they teach you goes beyond how to solve a maths question or write a good essay. At DA, you will learn the importance of hard work and discipline, lessons that will have untold value in the future. No matter where you are in your studies, the teachers at DA can help you to become better, both with your academic work and even as a person. Thank you so much to the teachers at DA for all they have done.
Harrison Nguyen - The King School - Year 12

Name: Harry Kha
Testimonial:
So, I joined DA just before the HSC Trial Examinations and I have to say, it was pretty scary at first. Not because the tutors were mean, but I was scared that my dumbness would not meet the tutor's expectations of me. I was averaging about 60% in maths and I have never felt so disappointed in myself. I needed to re-learn all of the 3u & 2u maths 
content in 4 months (it is a lot of content in 4 months. not as much as uni though c: ). It was hard work. However, that didn't change the fact that DA wasn't going to give up on me. They understood me and pushed me to my limits. They always had my back whenever I needed them, and I used their support to get me through the HSC examinations.  DA was like my second home, and the tutors there were like family. They loved us and we loved them. The tutors at DA brought the best out of me, and for that, I am truly grateful.
Harry Kha - Fairvale High School- Year 12

Name: Helen Au
Testimonial:
I’m Helen Au and I started D.A. Tuition when I was in Year 8. I decided to go to tutoring as my results for Maths were under average and I couldn’t pass any Maths exam. My first experience at D.A. Tuition was good and I felt like I was learning which was a new experience that didn’t happen in school. I had another tutor before Miss Stephanie and although she was good, I felt awkward in asking for help. When I started having Miss Stephanie, even for a short period, my results significantly improve as soon as I joined. My first test with D.A. Tuition, not only could I get over 50%, I got more than 80% which made me gain confidence and positivity in Maths. With Miss Stephanie, I wasn’t afraid of seeking help because even if I was scared, Miss Stephanie would ask if I needed help. Miss Stephanie is very kind, and she knows what her students need to improve. If she felt like I wasn’t trying my best, she would then encourage me to do my best for everything including my other subjects. Throughout my 2 years with Miss Stephanie’s teaching, I never got bad results and in year 9 for my Yearly exam I got 100%. I would have never thought that getting 100% for Maths was possible before going to D.A. Tuition and before having Miss Stephanie. Every tutor of D.A. Tuition has a different style of teaching. Even if you don’t see major improvements, other great and experienced tutors can help you.
Helen Au - Bonnyrigg High School - Year 9

Name: Helen Nguyen
Testimonial: DA has really changed how I approached my studies. The tutors there, especially Miss Lai, has always pushed and encourage me to do my best and provided a supportive environment where I felt comfortable to ask questions without feeling embarrassed. They continued to provide me with the best resources to help me understand content and always helped me develop my study habits. Although I have finished school, the relationships I’ve built with the tutors will always remain with me and remind me the impact they had on my studies throughout high school.

Name: Henry Pham
Testimonial:
Before I was introduced to DA Tutor, I couldn't strive towards a higher grade as I had no motivation around me and I still couldn't perform well with my studies. After I was introduced to DA tutor, I have found my way around my problems and identified my motivation to do better and more superior. Thus, DA tutor has helped me overcome my failure and gave me a second chance of succeeding. My marks improved from 60s into 80s and high 90s, therefore I am really thankful to DA tuition.
Henry Pham - Cabramatta High School  - Year 12

Name: Hoai Nguyen
Testimonial: Mr Danny is my role model since he is very special towards me and makes me laugh by calling me Jackie Chan.

Name: Hussein Al-Farawn
Testimonial: During my short tenure at DA tuition centre, I have been blessed with the opportunity to be taught under miss Linda. She has exponentially improved my previously ignorant brain, into a vast library, engulfed with intricate formulas and pushed me to my up most limits, challenging my intellect with every topic. Under her teachings, I have gone from a B average student to achieving 98% on my most recent test. I wholeheartedly believe that miss Linda has made a positive impact on both my life, and my knowledge regarding mathematics.

Name: Huyen Nguyen
Testimonial: I have had Mr Danny as my science teacher for about a year and a term now. Mr Danny is a helpful teacher and if I never turned to him for any of my assignments I’d probably fail science in general. Whenever I walk into class every Wednesday I come with a smile on my face expecting him to syringe me with helpful information.

Name: Isabella Huang
Testimonial:
Although I have only attended this tuition for a year and a half, it is truly undeniable how much improvement I have made over the year after attending DA as I go into my HSC exams!

During early year 11, I was barely scraping through both my Advanced and Extension 1 Math classes, especially extension, where I was getting marks just over 50%. However, Mr King's classes truly changed the trajectory of my academic performance, as his kind, patient teaching methods and engaging application of class work truly made learning math enjoyable for me. From then on, I noticed a significant jump in not only my performance but my overall confidence in myself going into exams and am now ranked 5th overall after my trial exams.
Also, I owe everything regarding 2U math to Mr Bunsea! Although I've only had him for a term, it is undeniable how much he has impacted me as a student! Mr Bunsea is incredibly nice in the way he teaches; he is incredibly patient and will always go through any problem step by step, whether it be a somewhat silly multiple choice question to the hardest question in a paper! Thanks to him (as well as Mr King), I was able to rank 1st in two of my assessments (including trials) and ranked 2nd in advanced overall.

Mr Danny's unwavering passion for Biology must not go unnoticed! Before joining his class, I was performing incredibly average in biology, with no truly outstanding results. Biology is a notoriously difficult subject, with its incredibly heavy content and even more difficult application within exams. However, Mr Danny taught biology in a way my school biology teacher could never; with passion. He made some of the most confusing concepts seem like a breeze, and there has never been a time where I walked out of my 5-7 class thinking, "man, I'm screwed." Not only this, his classes were super fun! It is all because of Mr Danny that my confidence in biology skyrocketed, as I now go into my HSC exams with a comfortable overall rank of 7th.

Finally, Mr Jonathan is by far one of the best english tutors ever!! English has always been one of my weaker subjects, however Mr Jonathan's unwavering commitment to our performance allowed me to excel in advanced english. I am truly thankful for the time and effort he put into providing thoughtful and constructive feedback as well as his bright and kind personality, for were it not for him, I most definitely would not have achieved the grades that I did!

Overall, I absolutely cannot recommend DA enough!! :)

Name: Isabella Luong
Testimonial:
I joined DA tuition a little over a year ago, and I must say that it has been an absolutely fantastic experience. I give full credits of my journey in DA to Mr Bunsea as my first tutor and current tutor his friendliness, sense of humour and his ability to break down while also injecting humour into the maths questions in particular strategies to allow me to understand and memorise complex questions in an easier way. He creates a positive and inclusive environment that encourages open discussions during our classes every lesson.

It is evident that he genuinely cares about his students as I remember one time he brought a box of donuts to share during our private classes and no one wanted to try them due to the size of the donuts, so he cuts the donuts into smaller pieces to let us all try his donuts.

The atmosphere of DA is also like family from the moments I walk into class I get greeted with warm smiles and genuine kindness. As I am going into year 11, subject selections created a stressful thought in my mind but my peers in my private class were able to explain into detail of what subjects would suit me best or worst. Their advice reduced my worrying and changed my ideas on particular subjects.

DA changed my perspectives on life and school in many different aspects while they helped me maintain my high marks in maths but also giving my family and I an opportunity with a new dog, when picking up my dog from DA the greetings of young children crowding my dog made me smile as I recognised the happy community DA really is and how happy all the students here are.

Name: Ivy Nguyen
Testimonial: Throughout undergoing DA, my overall performance improved significantly. Miss Linda, my teacher, is an incredibly dedicated educator who exhibits immense passion in bringing out the best in her students. I highly recommend her to everyone, although she’s THAT good that I might need to gatekeep her. SHOUTOUTT TO MY FAV Miss Linda for her support, which has greatly contributed to my academic progress and results.

Name: Ivy Nguyen
Testimonial: Coming to DA and transferring teachers, I was abit nervous but Ms. Kassandra has been an incredible support throughout my Year 12 journey, picking up the work that I was lost in. She’s a hardworking tutor who is passionate with her work as she keeps us focused on our work, always pushing us to do our best. Thanks to her guidance, my grades have improved significantly, and she’s helped me stay on track, even during the most stressful times. I truly appreciate everything she’s done to help me succeed. #slay

Name: Jaclyn Yin
Testimonial:

Name: Jacob Danh
Testimonial:
DA tuition is more than just an education centre rather a place that feels like home, as a former student who enrolled in yr 8 till yr 12 I can wholeheartedly say that DA surpasses all other tuitions. DA manages to continuously entertain students through a captivating canteen and reward system (they make my favourite frozen milk tea) while balancing enjoyment with a team of remarkably experienced teachers who are both well versed in their field of work but also adept at instilling discipline while maintaining a mutual respect with their students. From day one, I was welcomed with open arms, the teachers not only remembered my name at an instant but had no issues navigating me around at times when I had felt lost. Beyond caring for their students, DA places a significant emphasis on prioritising parents, ensuring they remain closely connected throughout their child's educational journey with regular updates via messengers and phone calls.

I would like to say that from previous experience, having tried out other tutorings like Troungs and Uplift, I very much prefer DA’s distinctive teaching style. What makes DA’s teaching style special is being able to provide welcoming classrooms with fewer students where the teachers constantly provide feedback and accommodate to student weaknesses, for example I’ve had many upcoming exams where my teacher Mr King would print out and organise resources made customed to my exam while organising additional days for me to come in and prepare prior to the examination. This learning environment compared to how Troungs and Uplift treat students as an additional number to their business by making sure each and every seat in the classroom is filled (average senior classroom consisting of 40 students) has proven to be favourable as students are often missing DA as they graduate and wanting to come back.

At DA, besides building strong lasting relationships with my teachers, being able to freely text my tutors whenever in need of help or fostered many friendships through such a great community, DA on the other hand has driven me to become a confident person with ambitious goals. With the countless amount of in-class activities, one on one competitions and academic races Miss Amanda and Linda have not only revolutionised teaching to become a fun and exciting form of learning but have shaped me to be a competitive individual who strives for perfection.

As I have finished my HSC I would like to thank Miss Amanda, Linda and Mr King who have mentored me helping me achieve a raw 96 for 2u. Miss Stephanie, my English teacher who has consistently motivated and encouraged me, drastically improved my performance from someone who absolutely dreaded and could not remember a single essay to someone who high band 5 english. For someone looking to finally enjoy tutoring in a place they call their second home, DA is definitely the best tuition.

Testimonial:
HSC is like a falling off a cliff. You will keep falling unless you start finding your way up. Believe me when I say this: more than 50% of your stress in Year 12 comes from the school itself, whether it is the competitive environment, or because your teachers push you non-stop; but by the time you reach the third term, the stress will tire you out. But don’t be too ceebs and wait for someone to lift you up. Be proactive! Worry about your grades? There are heaps of resources available to support your learning and refine your skills. Tutors are willing to teach you from scratch if you don’t understand a particular concept. Academic support is one thing, our tutors are also specialists at giving moral support. Here, the tutors will not give you unnecessary stress unless you ask them to, or you’re just really messed up! But they are always supportive and willing to give their best to help you cope with the stress. Btw, Year 12 is when most of you will be stuck on your careers options!!! Don’t hesitate to talk to your tutors. They all have experience with university and will give you their honest opinions about different courses and careers paths. So something to remember, talk to your tutors, study smart (don't study hard), and be bothered. Glhf.
Jade Do - Fairvale High School - Year 12

I learned to enjoy Maths a lot more- there really is an intricate beauty to problem solving. I was not Band 6-bound before I joined...but they helped me get there.

The staff are all so nice, and they help you to understand not only the course you're being tutored for, but how exactly the HSC works (which is always very confusing for students since schools usually don't teach us this, and often teachers don't understand how it works themselves) and what subjects you should focus on, according to your strengths and weaknesses and subject scaling, in order to maximise your ATAR.

Name: John Tran
Testimonial: As I write this review with tears falling from my eyes, I realise that my 2-year journey with DA has come to an end. My last two years of high school were made very enjoyable by DA, and my only regret is not joining earlier.

Name: Joie Lim
Testimonial:
I enrolled into DA tuition at the start of 2024 in my second term of year 12. I can confidently say that having a tutor was one of the best academic decisions I’ve ever made! This was made possible by the amazing teachers at DA, who gave me the resources I needed to reach my full potential in high school math and english. I’d also like to give a special thanks to my teachers Ms Kassandra and Mr Bunsea for ultimately helping me throughout year 12.

Mr Bunsea was my Maths Standard 2 tutor. He is an incredible teacher who is extremely patient and committed to his students growth and success. He teaches concepts in memorable, easy to understand ways and gave thorough answers to all of my questions, even if I asked him a question every 5 minutes he would never fail to give a detailed response. Additionally, he offered new problem-solving techniques that made challenging questions simpler to approach and faster to solve. Moreover, DA also has a ton of resources for maths such as flash cards, past trial papers and HSC questions (sorted by topics) and trial classes in preparation for assessments, trials and hsc. Providing me with the necessary practice with non-routine questions to build confidence in exams. With all of these resources and Mr. Bunsea's immense help I was able achieve truly incredible feats that I could never have imagined. After I joined DA I got 100% in both assessments 2 and 3. And in my math trials I got the highest score which was 97%. My rank had gone from a 13 in semester 1 to ranking 1st overall in my school’s maths standard course at the end of year 12.

My English Standard tutor was Ms. Kassandra. She is an incredibly sweet and caring person who is passionate about her students’ success and self-confidence in their English skills. English was definitely my weakest subject. To completely prepare for trials and HSC, Ms. Kass broke down the syllabus into topics that were easier to understand and significantly improved my writing abilities. Also giving us extremely helpful advice on how to get the maximum marks possible. Additionally, Ms. Kass shows a great deal of concern for her students' work because she consistently gives thorough feedback on all of my writing, something she does for all of her students, even if they were all turned in at the same time. The intense amount of practice on timed essay writings and feedback before trials and hsc made me the most prepared I had ever been for any of my exams. Since joining DA, my writing has significantly improved. I got a 97% for my Mod C assignment, and I am able to write full Band 5-6 essays in timed conditions. I ranked an overall 8th in my schools Standard English course.

Overall DA has given me the best chance of success in high school. With the incredible teachers and positive learning environment I was able to reach unimaginable heights.

Name: Lachlan Le
Testimonial: DA tuition is such a great place to learn, the teachers here are AWESOME and have really unique teaching techniques that help me understand whatever I'm learning easier such as Mr King, Miss Lai and Mr Danny. The reasons listed above is why DA tuition is the best tuition to sign your children up to.

Name: Lachlan Nguyen
Testimonial:
After being a Student here for three years I can definitely say that I am glad my parents enrolled me into this facility. DA tuition is a non like any other tutor facilities ive been enrolled to, the teachers here are very supportive and put in a lot of hard work into helping their students achieve the best results possible.
TEACHER REVIEW:
- Miss Linda: Miss Linda may seem intimidating at first but is actually nice and supportive and really cares about her students and strives to encourage her students to get the best results they can. Although she can be intimidating and a p(icky)erfectionist, she also has softer sides that make easy to tease, bribe and banter with, however when things get serious she can be VERY VERY SCARY... that said she is probably one of favourite mentors who I've had the honour of being her student in math. 10/10 would banter again
- Miss Stephanie: Miss Stephanie is the opposite, as in she is very nice and has a more approachable vibe. She's the kind of person that you'd feel bad for making them angry and is the angel's advocate. Above all else she like many other tutors in the facility is very encouraging and motivational when it comes to teaching her students. I am very glad that Miss Lai abandoned me. 10/10 would be a student again
-Miss Lai: Miss Lai is a combination both Miss Linda and Stephanie (they're pretty much the three musket tiers together). Although she abandoned me (which was understandable), my time with her was nothing but short and sweet. She is very knowledgeable and wise, puts in a lot of effort into helping her students. One of my best English tutors I've had the pleasure of being a student of. -10/10 for abandoning me :((
-Mr Danny: The devil himself El diablo. Mr Danny is a man that is not to be fooled by his looks although he may seem like a normal man with a contagious charisma. He is an unhinged man driven by the success and discipline of his students, as his way of supporting and motivating his students can be seen as degrading compared to other tutors. That said he is one of best tutors I've had his wackiness mixed with his sarcasm and occasional banter with his students make for an entertaining and insightful lesson as he does his best to ensure that his students understand the topics he is teaching by installing "discipline" into them. Overall best mentor 10/10 would recommend if you plan on selling your soul to the devil
"100 or be disowned"-Mr Danny
-Mr King: Don't let the cool teacher facade fool ya, under that is a mischievous teacher who preys on the suffering of his students in order for them to succeed, and by suffering I mean giving more work to his students than he needs to (which is what basically almost all the teachers do). A man who goes by "every man for himself" code. However pass all that he is a chill teacher who is very supportive and motivational in his own ways and much like the others can provide very insightful thoughts and life lessons all for the best interest of his students. 10/10 very chill and very cool

Name: Madison Eung
Testimonial: DA tuition is an amazing tutoring centre that has such motivating and supportive teachers who push you to do your best.
