Thanks to DA, I was able to improve significantly with the support of my tutors! :)

Name: Madison Eung
Testimonial:
Despite starting at DA towards the end of my time in highschool, I was able to significantly improve my marks and my outlook on academics. Before I started at DA, English marks were not very good and I lacked a lot of confidence in English exams. However, my marks and understanding about different themes and concepts in the syllabus improved significantly with the help of Ms Kassandra. She helped build my confidence with writing, and my ability to write essays and short answer questions in exam conditions. Her knowledge and understanding about how to efficiently complete exams (specifically paper 1),from her past experience helped me improve my performance in exams and helped me better understand the best way to tackle an English essay/ exam paper. Her support, dedication and teaching did wonders and I’m forever thankful for her!

Maths was one of my worst subjects at the beginning of year 11, but by the hsc, maths was my best subject. Mr King and Ms Amanda were amazing tutors who helped me go from 40% in my exams to a band 6 in advanced maths! Mr King helped me improve my confidence and understanding about topics, especially ones that I struggled to do. He also changed my perspective on studying and how hard work really pays off! Without him, I would have never done well in maths!! Additionally, Ms Amanda was able to break down the most difficult questions and topics which made it much easier for me to understand, thus making more simple questions easier to do. They both helped push me to excel in my exams by teaching me how to efficiently study and complete questions at record speed!

Overall my experience at DA was amazing and I believe the amazing tutors tremendously helped me in school with their teaching and support. DA become like a second home to me, and I definitely recommend DA to anyone who is looking for a tutor who will help them excel in their studies!

Name: Madison Huynh
Testimonial: I cannot recommend DA Tuition highly enough. Throughout my entire high school experience, their support has been invaluable. I truly appreciate all their hard work, and I am grateful for the confidence and skills I’ve gained thanks to their efforts.

Name: Marcus Cheong
Testimonial:
Being a student of Mr King’s at DA Tuition made me realise one thing: tutoring someone is extremely difficult. Not only do you have to be excellent at the subject you are teaching, you also have to be extremely skilled at teaching that subject, and you have to be patient, kind and understanding enough to create an environment where the student can learn effectively. After being taught by Mr King for several years, I can say with 100% confidence that Mr King is definitely all of those things and more. Week by week, I would come to my tutoring sessions with questions about maths problems that I was stuck on, and without fail, Mr King was able to figure out not only the best way to solve the problem but the best way to teach me how to get to the same solution as well. Mr King showed to me that he was more than just a tutor, but also a mentor. Often I would get lazy, and show up to class sheepishly admitting that I had barely even touched the homework. However, Mr King never got mad, and instead he would be stern, unimpressed and yet reassuring in such a way that I didn’t want to let him down next time. By the end of the year I achieved 1st in 2U Maths and 5th in 3U Maths at Fort Street High School, due largely in part to Mr King’s excellent tutoring. On top of everything, I enjoyed being a student of Mr King’s and I could not recommend him more as a tutor.
Marcus Cheong - Fort Street High School - Year 12

Name: Marcus Nguyen
Testimonial: Coming from my heart I can truely say what an amazing and eye opening experience I’ve had with DA Tuition, especially in the case of having Mr King and Mr Bunsea. Having these two incredible tutors at DA has really changed my school life and my ambitions towards my work. In the recent terms I’ve been working along side Mr Bunsea and Mr King with my 2unit and 3unit maths. Before joining DA I’d never had the marks that truly reflected the student I could become, nearly in the brink of failing 2unit and 3unit maths. My marks we average at best hovering around (50%-80%) for 2 and 3 unit maths. I was at a point in my life where I felt I was too far down the rabbit whole of failure and the pressure of failing was getting to me. At that point math was the thing I most hated about my life. My ego and ambition were crush in mid year 11 as I’d always been a high achiever in years 9 and 10 (getting around 85%-95%), and by the past experience I’ve had with DA I decided to join the tutor with little hopes that I could get back on track. Initially I had low expectations and assumed that I wouldn’t gain much value. I was soon proven wrong and realised that I had much to learn. DA not only taught me what I wanted to know but what I needed to know, and it really shaped me up as a student to set my goals higher and work harder towards them. I can proudly say that this term I was able to get perfect marks(100%) in both my 2unit and 3unit assessment and I had DA and Mr king and Bunsea. Now I stand as a complete different person compared to who I was before I joined DA, and I’m better than I ever have been. Now all my dreams seem possible and I have an extreme passion and love for maths. Joining DA was the best decision I made for my maths results but also for all my school subjects as I now realise that anything is achievable and I understand how it feels to truly work hard. I love maths, I love DA and I have Mr Bunsea and Mr king to thank for that. Thank you DA!!!

Name: Melissa Ly
Testimonial:
To the current and future students , tutors or DA tuition and to the very special Miss Amanda, I started out my experience with the amazing tutors at DA as a struggling maths student who found it difficult to learn new concepts and had a weak foundation with the basics of high school maths.
I entered GAT class with Miss Amanda, this moment was a turning point for me, in my mind it allowed me to see that this was another chance to achieve my goals with every exam that came up. It was in the two years where I worked my way up, boosting my marks range to 80-100% It was the first time in my whole high school life, where I achieved 100% on a 2unit exam! You know what else I’ve never dreamed of happening ever? Ranking 1st in a 2unit trial exam. I’m seriously not trying to boast, but the main point of this is that throughout all my struggles whether they’re academic or 
personal, Miss Amanda was always there to provide me with help. It’s because of that, that I made achievements that I could be proud of for as long as I live!
Also that one text conversation in 2015 … I think Miss Amanda’s wish came true I would read it every time I was feeling a bit lazy or before exams – I’d read it over and over again because it was really special to me.
And of course Miss Amanda. She said something that really stuck to me and encouraged me to work harder every time I thought of it.
“To be the best at something you have to love it.”
It stuck with me because honestly, I never really liked maths at first – in fact I hated it. But with the help of the right tutor you’ll start to see that its not as bad as you think (if you hated it like I did hahaha!) You should also probably keep in mind how important Miss Amanda’s constant reminders for you to work hard are. They stay in your mind all day and night.
Finally a special note to the current and future students of DA, I’d like to say that DA is one of the best tutoring places ever! You’ll never meet anyone as friendly, helpful and as welcoming and intelligent as the tutors at DA. They’ll always be there for you regardless of any matters that you have and I know that they treat every single student with all their care and compassion to make sure you guys put maximum effort into anything you set your mind to. I know for a fact that every single tutor puts in everything they can to make sure you’re happy and confident, so appreciate it cause I know I do!
I’m truly thankful for my experience here at DA because it helped me realised my passion for learning everything there is to know in the world, even if it’s the hardest thing to understand.
Melissa Ly - St Johns Park High School - Year 12

Name: Michelle Chand
Testimonial:
One thing that distinguishes DA is its staff. The teachers are not only there to help you learn, but they're also a network of support for when you are struggling with school and life, particularly through the tough times of the HSC. I remember when I was a student, Mr Bunsea and Miss Amanda always made me a priority, showing me how much they cared about me through their unwavering efforts in pushing me to work my hardest and try my best. And so, I did not only make myself proud, but also to make them proud of my achievements which was 97% for 2unit and 47/50 for 3unit. I knew that I could message them whenever I was having trouble with a particular question or concept and they would never fail to make sure I understood it. Even though my HSC is over, I will always have the unwavering support of DA that will follow me throughout all my future endeavours, and you will too!
Michelle Chand - Hurlstone Agriculture High School - Year 12

Name: Michelle Dam
Testimonial:
English was never an easy subject and I’m sure everyone would agree, but my time here at DA and with my English teachers have taught me the value of English.
My journey began with Ms Angie teaching me throughout my junior years, learning different concepts each time with a fun spelling bee session in the final ten minutes.  Although the work can be hard and challenging, I discovered the joy and excitement in coming to class to meet my teacher and do English. It was as simple as that. I just wanted to learn. 
I had a few transfers after Ms Angie and it was quite upsetting, but English continued the same with my new teachers (Ms Phuong and Ms Michelle). Then when my senior years hit, I was worried about my progress in English. Whether I was competent or not, I felt like English wasn’t for me anymore. I lost the motivation and joy I once had and it was really regretful. However, I met my current teacher Ms Lai who was a mother to me. She supported me and helped me push through the work I had. She gave me new words - new ideas that inspired me to work harder and do better. It has reached a point where in the future, I’d like to apply English into my future careers if possible. 
Now, I can definitely write essays with much more confidence in my techniques and structure. I can develop my own personal ideas and expand on it through different writing forms (imaginative, discursive, transcript). I can have the confidence to do well in assessments without having to compare marks to other people and feel discouraged about it. Instead, I focus on myself and improve on my mistakes. 
With DA teachers, this can really be achievable as long as you put in the effort to do the work. The more effort you put in, the more it pays off in the future. Thank you DA :)
Michelle Dam - Fairvale High School, Year 12

Name: Michelle Nguyen
Testimonial: I've only been at D.A Tuition for approximately 1.5 years and yet this place has increased my grades significantly in comparison to other places that I've studied at! My tutors - Mr Bunsea, Mr King, Mr Adem, Ms Amanda and Miss Kassandra have all played a significant role in my HSC journey from year 11 to 12. With Math, Mr Bunsea, Mr King and Ms Amanda continuous faith in my abilities to strive towards attaining the highest marks have pushed me into maintaining my marks within the A grade range, which was something that I didn't believe I could do. With English, Miss Kassandra has definitely been the perfect match for me as a tutor, she's incredibly caring and has worked tirelessly to ensure that my grades only continue to rise as my year 12 journey continues. With chemistry, Mr Adem is definitely the funniest yet smartest person I know for science. Testimonial:
When joining DA as a student in Year 12, I never would have imagined the gravity of how this place would impact my life for the better. Struggling with the pressures of Year 12, being in and out of hospital and surrounded by unhealthy friends at school, I gave up on my education, I gave up on my future but most importantly I gave up on myself. 2 months before my HSC, I had no hopes of gong to university and I fell into a vicious cycle of slipping grades and destructive relationships. Miss Amanda had noticed and immediately set up a meeting with myself and my parents. Without realising, that night was the pivotal point of turning things around. When everyone around me lost faith in me, Miss Amanda didn't. She was there for me every step of the way, constantly checking up on me and enlisting the help of an army of DA tutors to further help me. She guided me through all the stresses I had, in and outside of the classroom. She didn't just push for good grades but emphasised that I should always preserve and not so easily admit defeat when faced with hardships. Receiving an offer for Bachelor of Commerce and Bachelor of Laws felt like a dream. Only two months prior I was lost and strapped to a sinking boat. When I received the offer, something Miss Amanda told me came to mind. If you’re driving and you find yourself lost, instead of continuing and driving around in circles, pull over and ask for help, she and DA will always be there to give you the right direction.

Name: Nana tea

Name: Nathan Hoang
Testimonial: Ever since joining DA Tuition I have entered a environment where you are able to talk to your tudors openly about any issues regarding homework and assessments. Name: Nathan Lam
Testimonial:
“Are you telling me that I could earn as many mos as I wanted?” “No, I telling you that when you are truly ready, you don’t need to have mos.”
To be honest, that is a quote from a popular movie that I kind of change to suit what I thought helped me excel through DA, my school and most importantly, the HSC. A few years ago, I was at an old tutor, just being lackluster not really learning any information and there was no detention for me. Even after my schedule was changed to help me more, my parents decided to move me to a new tutor which is called DA tuition. I was introduced to a strange currency called mos, complete work and you’ll earn some, fail to do so and you are penalised. The scariest part to me is that if you run out of mos, you have to pay real money or spend detention which is worsen by their interest if you are late to pay. So I actually became more serious and always made sure that all of my homework is finished before the due date. It became a cycle for every week, completing homework, going to class, stockpile mos and repeat. It was the start of me getting the hang of Maths, I messaged with Ms Linda to help me on the subjects that I had to do at school.
My rank so far is only at a little above of the middle and at that time I thought it being impossible to reach the top. More than a year later, I was moved into a new classroom run by the top maths teacher of DA, Ms Amanda. She was very punishing on uncompleted homework, this made me work hard to insure that I don’t get her angry. We do have to pay a lot for solutions so I thought that my mos can help me be better at maths. For the first time of my life, I forced myself to become perfect, not being lackluster and push my knowledge of maths as hard as I can not to lose any mos I’ve worked so hard on. On the plus side, she did teach us the formulas at the clearest and showed us some tricks she had learnt. 2016 was the time where my ranking improved, it rocketed from steady middle to being THE TOP STUDENT OF 2 UNIT MATHS. Extension 1, I was always in the top 5, I can’t believed it back then.
I recognised my own knowledge that it suddenly started to expand like the universe and it didn’t stop here. Even though preliminary and year 12 was getting progressively challenging, my ever evolving mind was able to cope with the harder subjects. Every test I came on top plus the ranking, made my teachers proud and get paid a lot of mos. It started to feel strange that the mos didn’t made excited as usual but I felt the unbreakable ambition to becoming a god at maths. This is starting to get weird as I wanted to be truly ready for the HSC but I’ve begun to not care about the mos I gain anymore. For the rest of DA of being with Ms Amanda, I tried my hardest to succeed in the challenges she presents to us not to earn mos but to improve my area on maths. I kind of gained a reputation in her class as being the smart one, some students even asked me immediately to join their group in challenges.
HSC in 2017 was right around the corner, my rank was still at the top while Ms Amanda gave us double the class time to prepare us. Mos in this 2017 are not worth gaining anymore, the only thing a thousand times more valuable than it is my own developed mind. Full of creative thoughts and formulas, it is capable of solving a variety of complex math questions. During the HSC all the experience I’ve collected over the years came down to this moment. After the HSC my pile of mos became completely worthless to me and surprised me that I worked so hard for them. The results and ATAR came out which showed more than I expected, a 99 score on 2 unit Maths and 48/50 on extension 1 which is really ridiculous. I sent my results to DA and my teachers are very impressed, they asked me if I could write an inspiration for the younger generation. And I did, typing up my journey through DA and high school in a document that you are reading right now. For my useless mos, I’ve realized the true purpose of this currency. They are the training wheels of your education in DA that keeps you balanced and motivated on the course towards learning the fundamentals of maths until you are truly ready. Ready to learn the truth of the mos that they reveal the most priceless possession you already had, your potential to become the best damn person to wield such powerful knowledge. I wonder, had you ever figure out what movie I took a quote from?
Nathan Lam - Arthur Phillip High School - Year 12

Thank you to the teachers at DA, truly.
Work hard. Have a milk tea. Then work even harder.

Name: Quentin Nguyen
Testimonial: One of the most proper tutors in the area. as a former student of this tutor, this facility provides a network of support and resources to maximise your child's performance in school. started at this tutor at such a young age was the greatest investments towards education. thanks to Mr King, Mr Danny, Miss Lai and Miss Kassandra, i was looked after like a child to set me straight to focus in school and guide me to success in the future. as someone who is seeking a tutor for your child, DA TUITION is a MUST if you want your child to strive for success with their immaculate teaching skills.

Testimonial:
Throughout the years, we’ve all been through some rough patches. In my opinion, year 12 was definitely one of them. Balancing between work and school was stressful. I didn’t know what I was getting myself into. But because I was hesitant, I let myself continue to work in a negative environment. As a result, I felt anxious and unhappy with myself. However, due to the trust I’ve gained with my tutors over the years, they guided me into the right direction. I’ve come to a realisation that my marks didn’t define my worth. All in all, I don’t regret one bit about D.A. I’m super grateful for all the things they’ve done for me. They genuinely care about us so don’t be afraid to speak up. You are not alone!
Rachel Vo - St Johns Park High School - Year 12

Name: Rania Alfarawn
Testimonial: DA Tuition is a very welcoming environment. My tutors Mr Danny, Mr Bunsea, Mrs Jenny and Mr Jonathan have really help me with being ahead of what I’m taught at school. They are very resourceful and only want what’s best for you. They are always available and willing to go the extra mile. I only started going to DA towards the end of year 11 and when I started going to the centre I could tell that I was more confident in participating and answering questions in class due to knowing the knowledge before hand. All of my tutors teach differently but are able to adapt to better meet my needs. I 100% recommend going to DA Tuition and my only regret was not enrolling earlier.

Name: Ravneel Singh
Testimonial:
Mr King is very talented , supportive and patient tutor, who helped me achieve my full potential, in order to reach my ATAR! He helped me improve my grades immensely, and I only wish I started tutoring with him earlier on throughout my HSC years, as it really helped me enjoy and understand Mathematics! Which is rare!” 
Ravneel Singh - All Saints Catholic College - Year 12

Name: renmelngu
Testimonial: In my 6 years with DA it has truly served as one of the best tuition centers out there. My scores and grades have been constant of high marks. This year in math my scores have been nothing but constant improvements, recent exams better than the previous. Miss Linda has been the best motivating teacher and one of the best tutor. She is sweet,  kind, understanding and strives and pushes you do to your absolute best. Not to mention her lessons are engaging and entertaining, it’s hard to fall asleep in. I must say her way of teaching definitely works! My math exams last year were around 85-90% until this year where my grades skyrocketed from 90% last year to 97-100%! Furthermore, English isn’t my strongest subject and I’ve always hated the thought of it, not until now. Mr Danny is my current English tutor, he may seem a little harsh at times but that’s how he shows that he truly cares about you. Additionally, he has a good humour and maintains a well-balanced positive atmosphere. He has shown me another side of English I never knew existed, the fun side. Finding that “fun” side of English would help you to truly enjoy English, thus your marks would positively improve. Overall, DA encourages improvement, in all subjects, I 100% recommend it.

Name: Ronni Nguyen
Testimonial: From my perspective of going to DA TUITION it was a calm and enjoyable tutor I've been. The teachers are very kind, hardworking, charismatic and very positive. I've grown my English studies and getting use to new vocabulary. I would recommend people to join in for this TUITION for the future.

Name: Rosalind Bui
Testimonial:
I’ve experienced enrolling into various different tutors. But out of all of them, the most astonishingly impactful one has to be DA Tuition. I have been involved in this tutoring centre for nearly 2 years, and it has made quick progress in greatly contributing towards my academic journey.

Before my enrolment, the dreaded subject known as ‘Math’ was my absolute opposition during Year 9. Its numbers and concepts always greatly confused me, and my grades reflected my frustration. However, that all changed when I first met this particular tutor — a tutor equivalent to that of a saviour.

Miss Christina was more than just your average tutor; she was an outright lifesaver. As a person who is not really math-minded, her remarkable ability to break down complex concepts into simple, understandable terms was truly amazing. They didn't just teach me the material; they helped me understand it.

One of the things I appreciated most about Miss Christina was their patience. Unlike some past tutors I’ve met, they never made me feel stupid or discouraged, even when I struggled. They were always there to offer encouragement and support. With their help, I gradually began to see math in a new light.

Thanks to Miss Christina’s guidance, my grades skyrocketed through the ranks. I went from barely passing with 70s to consistently scoring grades ranging from at least 90 to 100 on my math tests. Their dedication and expertise were instrumental in my success. I am incredibly grateful for their help and would highly recommend them to anyone who is currently struggling with math.

Name: Rosy Trinh
Testimonial: This place is very good for my child. Teachers are friendly. Not too busy.

Name: Ruby Nguyen
Testimonial:
In the first term of year 12, I was struggling a lot for English and only received 16/20 for my assignment. After researching about different tutoring places, I was very impressed by DA's excellent reputation especially in private classes. Therefore, I decided to join and have never regretted my decision.

DA was truly a supportive environment for any students who want to strive harder and harder. The class size was small, so it was easy to study and to get a lot of individual help from the teachers. My English tutor, Ms Lai, has tremendously helped me throughout my whole year 12. Not only did miss spark my interest in English by introducing me into the insights of texts and pictures, miss Lai always checked up on me and comforted me whenever I felt stressed or insecure about my capabilities.

At the end, I was able to achieve the maximum score for every single assignment. Even more surprisingly, I was able to achieve a mark of 97 for my HSC and got 2nd in the state, boosting my ATAR up to 99.85.

I am always grateful of ms. Lai as well as DA for allowing me to achieve such amazing results. Therefore, I would strongly recommend DA for any students who want to improve and challenge themselves more.

Name: Ryan Ly
Testimonial: I joined D.A tuition around the end of last year and since then I’ve become a C student to an A student thanks to the help of D.A. Ms Stephanie and Ms Helen have guided me through this journey and will still continue to help me achieve my goals to the best of my ability. Not only is it beneficial for students, but the environment makes students very comfortable to commit to their learning too. My teachers have made me even more dedicated to trying my best and changed my mindset towards studying and learning. As a result of their proper guidance I actually enjoy Maths and English. D.A Tuition is truly one of the best tutors I have gone to and has substantially impacted my grades. Overall I highly recommend D.A tuition to students who want to make a change to their learning and preferably want guidance to achieve their goals.

Name: Ryan Ly
Testimonial:
I enrolled in D.A Tuition towards the end of last year, and the transformation in my academic performance has been nothing short of remarkable. Thanks to the dedicated support and guidance of Mr. Bunsea and Ms. Christina, I've evolved from being a consistent C student to achieving A grades. Their unwavering commitment to my progress is evident in the way they have mentored me, and I have every confidence that they will continue to help me excel in reaching my educational aspirations.

What sets D.A Tuition apart is not just the academic assistance it provides, but also the nurturing and supportive learning environment it cultivates. The profound impact of D.A Tuition is something I believe every student should have the opportunity to experience.

As a 2018 graduate of Macquarie Fields High School I am proud to have been a part of DA’s ET class. I had joined Da mid year 11 which resulted in a drastic increase in my school performance. Name: Ryder Ngo
Testimonial: Been a student at DA Tuition since year 5. The tutors are always optimistic and hardworking both in the centre and outside working hours to help with last-minute assignments/assessments. Mr King has helped reinforced my mathematics skills, making sure every step of a math problem is clear and easy to understand. Ms Lai always made sure that my essays for English were at top level, highlighting the weaknesses and strengths of my writing, helping me build better writing habits. Mr Danny has always helped me from the start of my journey at DA Tuition, perfecting the basics of math in year 5 all the way to the end of year 12 developing my knowledge in both biology and chemistry in an entertaining, engaging and optimistic way. DA has always been a comfortable learning environment and allowed me to grow as a student and a person. 10/10 would recommend DA Tuition ! :)

Name: Sam Sam
Testimonial: I've only had Ms Christine for a few months leading up to hsc but during that short time she has helped me tremendously. She was patient in hearing my ideas and gave helpful feedback that allowed me to improve significantly in time for hsc . Her support allowed me to gain confidence and make improvements I've been struggling with throughout the year in English Advanced. She was patient and reassuring and willing to go significant lengths for her students with a genuine passion to teach. I couldn't have done it without her. Thanks Ms Christine!

Name: Samantha Ly
Testimonial:
I first started going to tutoring when I was in year 3, I’ve been to countless tutoring centres. Most of them were boring, and all they cared about was the education. They never tried to interact with the students, not like the D.A does. I guess this is what made me hate tutor. My mum was always moving me to different centres, until my uncle told her about D.A. Once I was in that meeting, Miss Linda straight away convinced me and my mother that this might be the right centre for me. Miss Linda was my fourth Maths tutor teacher, to be honest at first I was scared of her, she seemed strict. But I shouldn’t have judged a book by its cover. After a while I got to know Miss Linda, her classes became more fun, she made work more engaging and interesting. Her Classes wasn’t always about work, most tutor teachers wouldn’t try to interact with their students, besides work related things. But Miss Linda was different, she would actually try to create conversation with me and my other classmates, which made it more fun. Miss Linda, always tried her best to make sure we got 90% and over in our tests. I personally say that I’m getting. During our class time, when she is teaching me something new she would often put it in a scenario, to make it more interesting or easier. When It came to our education, she was so determined for us to do our best, that she would write out pretty study notes with all her different colour pens and highlighters. Honestly this made me become more focused into the learning, because I even like to do those type of things during class and studying hours. Plus, it was so pretty. Although Miss Linda can be serious with MO’s, give a lot of homework, and ask for you to come back extra hours. It’s all for the positive benefits as us as a student and our education. (besides the MO’s part). All the hard work aside, Miss Linda is a caring, smartest, and determined Maths tutor teacher that I have ever meet. And I wouldn’t mind having her for another year or for my rest of my tutoring experiences. Plus, she makes amazing milk tea.
Samantha Ly - Fairvale High School, YEar 10

Name: Sami Ho
Testimonial:
Taking the first step in applying for DA, has been one of my better decisions I have made for myself and for my academics during the HSC. If there is one thing I regret, it would be not joining DA at an earlier period during my schooling years. Instead, I joined at a time I thought I really needed some guidance and support for my learning in Advanced English; a subject I was advised to drop before I entered year 12 by my school's English department. Big thank you to Miss Lai for her constant encouragement and disregarded the negative feedback I received from my school teachers; being a hopeless case if I chose to remain in Advanced. Her unbelievable patience and unwavering support shaped the confident writer I was during HSC, and even today. Advanced English, although was once my worst performing subject, became one of the subjects that consistently earned high marks.
I will be forever grateful for all that you have done for me in year 12 that has obviously shaped the person I am today with my academic achievements. I will forever be puzzled at how you were able to see my potential when others, including myself, have lost hope in me. Not only were you my English tutor, but a close mentor that helped me find peace with the subject instead of it being an ongoing war I often retreated from.
Unlike any tutor you will ever come across, the staff at DA are academic tutors as much as a friend who truly wants the best for you.
Sami Ho - Mary Mackillop Catholic College - Year 12
Theme: I Regret Not Joining DA Earlier

Name: Sami Nguyen
Testimonial: I've been to a few tutors before and DA is by far my favourite. DA has such a welcoming and friendly learning environment. Ive had Ms Anna for a few years now, shes been really helpful with my maths and very patient which i am greatly thankful for. Ms Stephanie has greatly improved my English marks ever since joining her class last year. Shes helped me rediscover my love for English with her insightful feedback and kind encouragement. Thanks for everything Ms Anna & Ms Stephanie, I am forever grateful.

Name: Samira Abdurahman
Testimonial:
For me, having already earned satisfactory marks in most of the subjects at school, I felt that going to tutoring would be unnecessary for me.  But here at DA, I learnt that doing well at school isn’t just about getting an A for all your assignments, it’s about putting in strenuous effort beforehand and striving to get best outcome possible for you. Thanks to Ms Linda, I’m now more productive with all my work and I now aim to get the highest results in my class rather than just a satisfactory mark. Although only being here at DA for only 2 terms, I’ve noticed an improvement in my grades, but most importantly, I’ve now got more extensive knowledge on topics that I’ve previously had trouble with and my work ethic has increased dramatically.  
Samira Abdurahman - Moorebank High School - Year 9

Name: Sandy Tran
Testimonial:
I would always have low confidence in my grades since it was always average. But, since the day I came here, Miss Linda has  taught me that your grades do not define who you are. Having low grades does not mean you're unintelligent, as long as you improve. Everyone here cares for you and will do anything to help. 
Sandy Tran - Cabramatta High School, Year 9

Name: Sanju John
Testimonial: Highly recommend tutoring.Very dedicated staff.Overall very good in all subjects…Unlike many other tutoring services not really only money focused and student achievement is their priority.

Name: Sarah Cheav
Testimonial: DA is a very helpful tutor and has helped to improve my grades a lot . Miss Stephanie is one of the many friendly tutors who have helped me achieve full marks in my latest English test. Mr King is also another of the many great tutors who has made maths a lot more enjoyable and simple.

Name: Sarah Chheav
Testimonial: DA is a tutor like no other. All the teachers are very friendly and always willing to help. Lessons are always fun and have helped to improve my grades a lot. Since beginning at DA, my English tutor, Miss Kass and Math tutor Mr King always made sure that I understood concepts well and have helped to improve my habits towards leaning by making maths and English enjoyable every lesson.  They have always been there for me when I needed help understanding difficult topics where instead of being discouraged from learning, they have changed my attitude for the better by being patient and passionate in their teaching

Name: Sasha Cio
Testimonial: Miss Kassandra is the best english tutor I have ever had. After having her for the past year I can say she has had a positive impact on my english marks. For my first year 11 Extension English 1 task she helped me achieve a 90%. I am grateful to have found such a compassionate tutor who supports and guides me towards achieving exceptional results.

Name: Sasha Cio
Testimonial:
I’ve been at DA for 3 years now, and during this time, I’ve received endless support from all my tutors, leading to a dramatic improvement in my overall grades.

Since I started at DA, I’ve had Ms. Kassandra for English, and I couldn’t be more grateful. She is the kindest and most understanding tutor I have ever had, always supporting my English endeavors. Under her guidance, I’ve maintained a 90+ average in 4U English. She consistently takes the time to help me understand the texts I’m studying and further improve my writing through her unwavering support.

At the beginning of Year 12, I started math tutoring with Mr. Bunsea. His passion for math has helped me find a new found appreciation for the subject, drastically improving my overall performance. Thanks to his endless patience and effective teaching methods, I’ve been able to thoroughly understand all my topics and scored 97% on my latest exam.

Mr. Danny has been an outstanding tutor for business studies. His engaging approach to teaching has helped me thoroughly understand the business syllabus and aim high for my results. From averaging 80% in my preliminary exams, Mr. Danny has helped me raise my score to 94% in my last HSC task.

Some other remarkable tutors I’ve had the privilege of learning from include Ms. Anna in Year 10, and Mr. King and Miss Amanda in Year 11. Their efficient teaching methods helped build a strong foundation for my future academic performance.

Overall, DA has been instrumental in helping me pursue my goals and enhance my academic performance. I wholeheartedly recommend DA Tuition to students seeking tutors who go above and beyond to improve their education.

Name: Savina Sivannarith
Testimonial:
D.A Tuition has been a game changer for me, and I can't say enough good things about Mr. Bunsea.
From the very first session, it was clear that he is a hardworking and dedicated math tutor who truly cares about his students' success. He takes his time to ensure I understand every concept, breaking things down into manageable pieces. His patience and commitment to my learning have made a huge difference in my confidence and skills.
Thanks to Mr. Bunsea's outstanding support, l've aced multiple math tests, including this yearly exam. With his guidance, l've been able to tackle even the toughest topics with ease. Every lesson feels like a step forward, and I can see my progress reflected in my grades. Without his help, I wouldn't have been able to make accelerated maths. I'm proud to say that l've always been getting straight A's on my report for math, and it's all thanks to the solid foundation he's helped me build.
The level of care Mr. Bunsea provides is truly inspiring. He goes above and beyond to create a supportive learning environment where I feel comfortable asking questions and making mistakes.
His belief in my abilities motivates me to push myself further and strive for excellence. If you're looking for a tutor who genuinely invests in your success, look no further than D.A Tuition and Mr.
Bunsea. He has not only helped me excel academically but has also inspired me to love math!

Name: Selena Cao
Testimonial: Very fun and friendly people. I get tutored for Chem, English and Maths. Mr Oliver explained things really well for Maths and Chemistry. This allowed me to come 3rd in the whole grade for Chemistry at school. I have Ms Kassandra for English and got As for the first time in 4 years. I'm very happy with my results after coming to DA and hope to get Band 5/6 for HSC. :D

Name: Selene Dixon
Testimonial:
I cannot express enough how truly remarkable my experience has been with Mr. Danny, my biology tutor at DA who has turned my academic life around. When I first approached him, I was on the brink of failing my biology class, with failing grades and a sense of hopelessness. Fast forward to my recent triumph in the trials where I secured the coveted first place, and I owe it all to Mr. Danny. From the very first session, it was clear that Mr. Danny was not your average tutor. His passion for biology was palpable, and it was infectious. He didn't just teach the subject; he brought it to life. He used creative teaching methods and real-world examples to make complex biological concepts understandable and engaging. His ability to simplify the most intricate topics was astounding.

Furthermore, I can hardly believe the transformation that has occurred in my Mathematics Standard 2 Course, all thanks to the incredible tutoring prowess of Mr. Bunsea. What sets Mr. Bunsea apart is his unwavering commitment to his students' success. He didn't just teach math; he demystified it. He broke down complex problems step by step, ensuring that I not only solved them but understood the underlying principles. His patience was unwavering, and he encouraged questions, no matter how ‘stupid’ they may have seemed. Mr. Bunsea’s dedication extended far beyond our tutoring sessions. He went the extra mile, providing additional resources, practice problems, and personalized study strategies. He was always accessible, ready to assist with homework or clarify doubts, even outside our scheduled sessions. His level of commitment was truly exceptional. The results of his tutoring are nothing short of astonishing. Going from a failing grade (averaging 40%) to securing the top position in the trials with a remarkable 91% is a testament to Mr. Bunsea’s exceptional teaching abilities. Additionally, achieving an assignment mark of 99 and my final ranking 6th out of 100+ students showed that his guidance not only improved my math skills but also rekindled my love for the subject.

For English Advanced, one of the standout aspects of Mr. Jonathan’s tutoring was his unwavering patience and encouragement. He created a safe and supportive environment. His feedback on my writing was constructive and inspiring, pushing me to constantly improve. He provided additional reading materials, practice exercises, and recommended literary works that expanded my horizons and deepened my understanding of English. His commitment to my success was evident in the countless hours he spent reviewing my assignments and providing valuable insights. In the end, the results were nothing short of astounding. From ranking last in prelim, to achieving consistently 1st place in all assessments and trials, with an overall rank of 1st in English. Mr. Jonathan’s exceptional teaching, mentorship, and guidance had propelled me to unimaginable heights.

Name: Selina Ho
Testimonial:
One term was all it took for me to realise that education is a beautiful gift and that it most certainly can be fun!

I've been through many places, and no other place hit home and felt comforting to return to every single week. It's all thanks to the staff who take their part putting 110% effort into the students to push them to do better. If it weren't for them, I wouldn't have ever found a place where I could embrace my joy to learn in a comforting environment.

They do their best in everything, pushing students in academics, helping students in school and outside of school, and being positive role models to the seniors and the younger ones.

As for me, I still will look up to my amazing math tutor Mr Bunsea. He's a great guy that really knows his stuff! I've never seen someone who is so happy to teach maths ever. Thank you to him, I've found myself comfortable in liking maths more as a subject. (Used to be an English kid, but he's really good at teaching, it completely flipped my perspective). I still hope to learn more from him and many other tutors, because I sincerely do look up to them as role models, as of now and as of the emerging future to come.

[ thank you mr bunsea for your encouraging messages!! they're truly the most motivating words i've heard as a student :) ]

Name: Serina Huang
Testimonial:
DA Tuition is a great tuition centre to go to for kids of all ages. All tutors will provide their utmost attention and care to you.
DA provided me a safe space to learn and build my skills for life and was especially helpful in senior years, providing me with an endless supply of resources. My senior tutors, Mr King, Mr Bunsea, Mr Danny and Ms Selina were always keen to help me with my questions and were always enthusiastic to help me achieve the best marks possible. And even after some dissapointing results, they were always there to pick me back up and strive to improve constantly.
Mr King, my extension one maths tutor, is always supportive and brightens my day. He is so commited to teaching, even responding to my texts at 2AM for a maths question or an assignment.
Mr Bunsea, my extension two maths tutor, is very passionate for maths and helped me through the challenging course. He always said that a certain topic was easy, easing my worries and always pushed me to be better.
Mr Danny, my biology tutor, was honestly someone I initially took a bit of time to warm up to. His lessons always had moments where he got the whole class laughing. As I had lessons with him throughout the years, I realised how much he values his students and their education.
Ms Selina, my english tutor, is very efficient and will always mark your work as soon as possible. She was really friendly while always being critical.
There are many other tutors that have taught me throughout my schooling life that I haven't mentioned, but they always were so caring and passionate about bringing out the best of a student's ability.
Thank you DA for the experiences and memories I had, always a good laugh and learning <3

Name: Sharnia Kumar
Testimonial:
One book, one pen, one child and one teacher can change the world. I have been going to DA for multiple years and have had the experience of being tutored by many different teachers at DA. All DA Tuition teachers have been very big role models in my multiple years of being at this tutor. My tutor improved my academic performance, work and study habits, my attitude towards learning and school and he encouraged independence and responsibility. My tutor was also able to get to know mine and many other students individual learning style and he adapted he’s teaching methods accordingly. He was not just my tutor but also a role model and mentor that has made a positive difference in my life. Not only has my tutor made my marks grow but he has made me grow as a person giving me the best gift of all, believing in me. 
Sharnia Kumar - Cecil Hills High School - Year 12

Name: Shazia Mohammed
Testimonial: All the teachers are very good and they know what they are doing and teaching I had Ms Jessica for English for 2 years and she is a amazing tutor and she really helped my grade from a C/B to a A and she was really fun to work with. I had Ms Anna for a year and she was a wonderful maths tutor and she also help my grade form 70%/80% to 90%+. Amazing tutor and a amazing place

Name: Sid Ragonton
Testimonial: I've just joined DA this year and Miss Helen has helped me adapt to this new setting and also helped to improve my math grades significantly!

Da tuition has been my home since year 3 and I'm about to enter HSC, It's the number 1 leading tutor place that supports, cares and educates their students not as teacher but as mentors. They are fully equipped in maths, english, chemistry, biology, business studies, legal studies and etc.

Miss Linda (Maths) constantly challenges and motivates me towards an higher mark or rank as she believes and caters my lesson plans so I able to reach my full potential.

Da tuition is an high tutoring institution that cares, motivates and pushes their students to achieve their fullest potential.

Name: Sophia Vo
Testimonial:
I've been to many many different tutoring places, but none of them hit as same as DA. When I first joined, I had Miss Stephanie, Mr King, and Mr Danny. They were all amazing teachers who helped me in all the areas I needed help with.

She would always find a way to get rid of the awkward atmosphere, making our entire english private class close friends with each other.

Would totally recommend to friends, 10/10.

Name: Sophie Vo
Testimonial:
Mr King has completely changed the way I think of tutoring. He makes everything easy to understand and breaks down any topic I’ve had trouble with to its very core. He is very patient with his students and nothing becomes boring with him.

If you’re one to struggle with memorising math formulas, definitely request Miss Amanda; it’s pretty hard to forget a formula once she teaches you it with her quirky stories. Enjoy your time there, try your best and don’t be dramatic, the workload isn’t THAT much. We’ve all been through it but worse. Just remember you can’t do well if you’re not willing to put the effort in it. Plus the more work you do, the more MOs you get which you can then buy drinks with so it’s really just a WIN WIN situation hehe.

Name: Tyson Pham
Testimonial:
I am thrilled to reflect on my remarkable six-year journey at DA Tuition, where Mr Bunsea, Miss Anna and Miss Jenny have been instrumental in shaping my academic success.

Mr Bunsea’s expertise in 3U maths was truly remarkable. His ability to simplify complex mathematical concepts and provide real world applications made the subject more accessible. Mr Bunsea's enthusiasm for the subject was contagious, and his dedication to ensuring that I grasped each topic thoroughly was evident in every lesson. Thanks to his guidance, I not only improved my understanding of 3U Maths but also developed a newfound appreciation for the subject, ultimately securing a B6.

Miss Anna's guidance in 2U Maths was equally exceptional. Her systematic and clear explanations helped me navigate through challenging topics with confidence. Miss Anna's use of visual aids (her notes🤩) and practical examples added a valuable dimension to the learning experience. She was always approachable and willing to go the extra mile to ensure that I mastered the mathematical concepts required for success, ultimately contributing significantly to my achievement of a B6 in the subject.

Finally, Miss Jenny's approach to teaching English was nothing short of brilliant. Her passion for literature and language translated into engaging lessons that made the subject come alive. Miss Jenny's attention to detail and personalised feedback on my essay writing significantly improved my skills. Her encouragement and patience created a supportive environment where I felt comfortable asking questions and seeking clarification. This transformative journey propelled me from my humble beginnings as an English Standard student in Year 11 to the pinnacle of academic achievement—a remarkable B6 in English Advanced.

I am genuinely grateful to the DA Tuition team, particularly to Mr. Bunsea and Miss Anna and Miss Jenny for their unwavering dedication to my academic growth. Their collective impact was instrumental in my journey towards becoming an HSC all-rounder. I wholeheartedly recommend DA Tuition to anyone seeking top-notch tutoring services that go beyond just grades and foster a comprehensive understanding of the subjects :)

Name: Zachary Peuo
Testimonial:
When I started my HSC I always felt pressure to perform but lacked motivation. This is mainly due to me not thinking I could achieve a high enough ATAR to get into my course. The only subject I felt highly confident in was math because of the help from my tutor at DA. My tutor always convinced me about how easy maths was and always made maths enjoyable for me. Although it wasn't easy, my maths mark would always fluctuate and I would be sad when I didn’t come first. When I got undesirable results, my tutor would motivate me again and remind me of what I should be doing and encourage me to keep trying. At times it felt like all my hard work at DA for all my maths packages had come to a waste because my school exam didn’t really match up to it but Miss would consistently remind me that I was working for the HSC and it would all pay off in the end. I never doubted her because of the outstanding results that she brought out for the students in the past so I just did what she told me to do and as a result I scored 100% in my school trials and 99%. For my HSC I achieved 80s for all my subjects that I worked really hard in, some I would've thought I would've got into the 90s but didn’t. But for maths 2u I achieved 99% and for 3u, 98%. Surprisingly this exceeded my expectations, and disregarding the fact that I got band 5s for all my other subjects, I still got into Optometry with my maths marks. This is an even better course than I would have ever dreamt of.  So guys, if you feel discouraged and things don't turn out the way you want, don't give up and keep believing in your tutors because if it doesn't work out at the beginning, it will eventually, because HSC is a long journey. 
Zachary Peuo - Prairiewood High School - Year 12

Name: Zahra Karaki
Testimonial: Miss Linda and Miss Amanda are the best math tutors I have ever had. I've experienced years of learning topics to ace my school exams and both definitely played a substantial part in my school exam results.
